Iwashita, originally from Toyotomi Village, recounted encountering the helmet in 1932 and facilitating its transfer to the Imperial Household Museum (now the Tokyo National Museum). [[File:Western areas Kofu Basin,from the Ouzuka-Kofun.jpg|thumb|left|500px|View of the northwestern Kōfu Basin from Ōzuka Kofun (Photographed in August 2013).]] {{-}} == References == * Hidenori Okano, "Ōzuka Kofun," ''Yamanashi Prefectural History: Archaeology Volume 1'' * Sadawo Iwashita, ''Now I Speak Out: The Horned Helmet Excavated from Ōzuka'', privately published, 2005. * Sukenobu Sueki, "On the Horned Helmet Excavated from Ōzuka Kofun," ''Journal of Yamanashi Archaeological Association, Issue 18,'' 2008. * ''Kai's Black Horse - Horses that shaped History,'' Yamanashi Prefectural Museum, 2014. ** Akihiro Morihara, "Did the Master of Kai-Chōshizuka See Horses?" == notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.chuo.yamanashi.jp/sougou/bunkazai.php?id=36 Yamanashi Prefecture Chūō City Comprehensive Guide: Ōzuka Kofun] * [http://www.manabi.pref.yamanashi.jp/db/servlet/dbview?id=A810000413 Yamanashi Navi Net designation1 Intypeation: Ōzuka Kofun] * [http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/kouko-hak/special/special-08haru.html Yamanashi Prefecture Archaeological Museum] (Exhibit on keyhole tombs from their emergence to disappearance) {{Japanese-history-stub}} * [[Isezuka Kofun (Chūō)]] ...Kofun]] ([[kofun]]) located in Takabe, {{ill|Chūō, Yamanashi|ja|中央市|12=draft}}, Yamanashi Prefecture. The tomb is classified as a [[circular burial moun ...gside nearby Kofuns like the {{ill|Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)|ja|王塚古墳 (中央市)|12=draft}}, it constitutes part of the Uyamadaira Kofun Group. revision.comment: Text replacement - "{{Kofun navbar}}" to "[[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]]"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Ōzuka Kofun | location = Ōtorii 4256, Chūō City, Yamanashi Prefecture[http://www.city.chuo.yamanashi.jp/sougou/gappei/data/meeting2_21.pdf Regarding the Handling of Social Education] - Tamaho Town, Tatomi Town, Toyotomi Village Merger Council | coordinates = {{Coord|35|34|46.6|N|138|33|15.1|E|map_size:20000_region:JP-19_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = Keyhole-shaped kofun | built = Early 5th century | area = Diameter: 40 m, Height: 7 m | burial = | burial goods = Iron axe | designation1 = City-Historic Site1 historical site | image = [[File:Ouzuka-Kofun Old tomb Chuo-City from southeastern side.JPG|280px]]
Ōzuka Kofun (Photographed in August 2013) }} [[File:Ouzuka-Kofun Old tomb Chuo-City from west side.JPG|thumb|280px|Ōzuka Kofun, view from the west (Photographed in August 2013)]] '''Ōzuka Kofun''' (王塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun ({{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}}) located in {{ill|Chūō City|ja|中央市|12=draft}}, Yamanashi Prefecture. Its type is debated as either a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} or a scallop-shaped kofun. == location and Historical Context == Ōtorii in Chūō City lies on the southern edge of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|12=draft}}, at the western end of the {{ill|Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵|12=draft}}, on a plateau called Uyamahira bordered by the Shichisato and Asari rivers. The site is 342 meters above sea level. This area hosts numerous archaeological sites from the {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代|12=draft}} through the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代|12=draft}} and {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft}}, typeing the Uyamahira Kofun Cluster with other mounds such as {{ill|Futagozuka Kofun|ja|二子塚古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Isezuka Kofun (Chūō City)|ja|伊勢塚古墳 (中央市)|12=draft}}. The mound is scallop-shaped but has also been evaluated as a keyhole-shaped kofun. It measures approximately 64 meters in length and 7 meters in height, and it is estimated to have been constructed in the late 5th century. == Excavations and burial goods == The posterior mound has been subject to looting, and later investigations confirmed a vertical pit-style stone chamber. The tomb was discovered in 1930 (Shōwa 5) and excavated by Yoshio Nishina. It was Historic Site1 a cultural property of {{ill|Toyotomi, Yamanashi|ja|豊富村 (山梨県)|12=draft}} in 1970 (Shōwa 45) and is now a city-Historic Site1 cultural property of Chūō City. Excavated burial goods include iron weapons such as swords, knives, and arrowheads, as well as armor, horse fittings, and cylindrical haniwa. The {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft}} houses a “{{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|12=draft}}” said to have been unearthed in Toyotomi Village. burial goods from the tomb include F-shaped bridles, with other horse fittings from the same era found at the {{ill|Kankan-zuka Kofun|ja|かんかん塚古墳|12=draft}} in the Nakatō Kofun Cluster, part of the Sone Hills, which are considered the oldest horse fittings in the Tokai region from the late 5th centuryMorihara (2014), p.27. Additional burial goods include elliptical bridle pieces from {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster (Ichikawa Misato Town)|ja|大塚古墳群 (市川三郷町)|12=draft}}. In Yamanashi Prefecture, horse teeth excavated at the {{ill|Shiobe Ruins|ja|塩部遺跡|12=draft}} in Kōfu City and at the {{ill|Higashiyama-kita Ruins|ja|東山北遺跡|12=draft}} suggest the arrival of horses in the late 4th century. The construction of small to mid-sized mounds in areas outside the Sone Hills from the late 4th century to the emergence of horse fittings in the 5th century reflects potential social transtypeationsMorihara (2014), p.28. == Significance of Ōzuka Kofun == Ōzuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 5th century based on its burial burial goods. Though not thoroughly investigated, its vertical pit-style stone chamber and scallop-shaped or keyhole-shaped type make it a notable example of ancient tombs in the region. Kofun construction in Yamanashi Prefecture began in the mid-4th century with large tombs like the {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|12=draft}} in the Sone Hills and shifted to smaller-map_size tombs throughout the Kōfu Basin by the mid-to-late 5th century. Ōzuka Kofun is part of this transitional phase, contemporaneous with other mounds such as {{ill|Kitsunezuka Kofun|ja|狐塚古墳|12=draft}} in Fuefuki City and {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun (disambiguation)|ja|大塚古墳|12=draft}} in Ichikawa Misato Town. This period was marked by increased militarization across Japan, driven by geopolitical tensions on the Korean Peninsula and the eastward expansion of the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト王権|12=draft}}. Tombs in Yamanashi during this era began to feature military-related burial goods such as armor. == Legacy of the “Horned Helmet” Attributed to Ōzuka Kofun == In 2005 (Heisei 17), a privately published booklet titled ''Now I Speak Out: The Horned Helmet Excavated from Ōzuka'' by Sadawo Iwashita was released. Iwashita, originally from Toyotomi Village, recounted encountering the helmet in 1932 and facilitating its transfer to the Imperial Household Museum (now the Tokyo National Museum). [[File:Western areas Kofu Basin,from the Ouzuka-Kofun.jpg|thumb|left|500px|View of the northwestern Kōfu Basin from Ōzuka Kofun (Photographed in August 2013).]] {{-}} == References == * Hidenori Okano, "Ōzuka Kofun," ''Yamanashi Prefectural History: Archaeology Volume 1'' * Sadawo Iwashita, ''Now I Speak Out: The Horned Helmet Excavated from Ōzuka'', privately published, 2005. * Sukenobu Sueki, "On the Horned Helmet Excavated from Ōzuka Kofun," ''Journal of Yamanashi Archaeological Association, Issue 18,'' 2008. * ''Kai's Black Horse - Horses that shaped History,'' Yamanashi Prefectural Museum, 2014. ** Akihiro Morihara, "Did the Master of Kai-Chōshizuka See Horses?" == notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.chuo.yamanashi.jp/sougou/bunkazai.php?id=36 Yamanashi Prefecture Chūō City Comprehensive Guide: Ōzuka Kofun] * [http://www.manabi.pref.yamanashi.jp/db/servlet/dbview?id=A810000413 Yamanashi Navi Net designation1 Intypeation: Ōzuka Kofun] * [http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/kouko-hak/special/special-08haru.html Yamanashi Prefecture Archaeological Museum] (Exhibit on keyhole tombs from their emergence to disappearance) {{Japanese-history-stub}} * [[Isezuka Kofun (Chūō)]] ...Kofun]] ([[kofun]]) located in Takabe, {{ill|Chūō, Yamanashi|ja|中央市|12=draft}}, Yamanashi Prefecture. The tomb is classified as a [[circular burial moun ...gside nearby Kofuns like the {{ill|Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)|ja|王塚古墳 (中央市)|12=draft}}, it constitutes part of the Uyamadaira Kofun Group. The name "Isetsuka" ... 5 KB (599 words) - 22:23, 14 January [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:History of Chūō City]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:City-Historic Site1 historical sites in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:王塚古墳 (中央市)]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:to be resorted]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)]] [[ja:王塚古墳 (中央市)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)}}

The mound is scallop-shaped but has also been evaluated as a keyhole-shaped kofun. It measures approximately 64 meters in length and 7 meters in height, and it is estimated to have been constructed in the late 5th century. == Excavations and burial goods == The posterior mound has been subject to looting, and later investigations confirmed a vertical pit-style stone chamber. The tomb was discovered in 1930 (Shōwa 5) and excavated by Yoshio Nishina. It was Historic Site1 a cultural property of {{ill|Toyotomi, Yamanashi|ja|豊富村 (山梨県)|12=draft}} in 1970 (Shōwa 45) and is now a city-Historic Site1 cultural property of Chūō City. Excavated burial goods include iron weapons such as swords, knives, and arrowheads, as well as armor, horse fittings, and cylindrical haniwa. The {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft}} houses a “{{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|12=draft}}” said to have been unearthed in Toyotomi Village. burial goods from the tomb include F-shaped bridles, with other horse fittings from the same era found at the {{ill|Kankan-zuka Kofun|ja|かんかん塚古墳|12=draft}} in the Nakatō Kofun Cluster, part of the Sone Hills, which are considered the oldest horse fittings in the Tokai region from the late 5th centuryMorihara (2014), p.27. Additional burial goods include elliptical bridle pieces from {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster (Ichikawa Misato Town)|ja|大塚古墳群 (市川三郷町)|12=draft}}. In Yamanashi Prefecture, horse teeth excavated at the {{ill|Shiobe Ruins|ja|塩部遺跡|12=draft}} in Kōfu City and at the {{ill|Higashiyama-kita Ruins|ja|東山北遺跡|12=draft}} suggest the arrival of horses in the late 4th century. The construction of small to mid-sized mounds in areas outside the Sone Hills from the late 4th century to the emergence of horse fittings in the 5th century reflects potential social transtypeationsMorihara (2014), p.28. == Significance of Ōzuka Kofun == Ōzuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 5th century based on its burial burial goods. Though not thoroughly investigated, its vertical pit-style stone chamber and scallop-shaped or keyhole-shaped type make it a notable example of ancient tombs in the region. Kofun construction in Yamanashi Prefecture began in the mid-4th century with large tombs like the {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|12=draft}} in the Sone Hills and shifted to smaller-map_size tombs throughout the Kōfu Basin by the mid-to-late 5th century. Ōzuka Kofun is part of this transitional phase, contemporaneous with other mounds such as {{ill|Kitsunezuka Kofun|ja|狐塚古墳|12=draft}} in Fuefuki City and {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun (disambiguation)|ja|大塚古墳|12=draft}} in Ichikawa Misato Town. This period was marked by increased militarization across Japan, driven by geopolitical tensions on the Korean Peninsula and the eastward expansion of the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト王権|12=draft}}. Tombs in Yamanashi during this era began to feature military-related burial goods such as armor. == Legacy of the “Horned Helmet” Attributed to Ōzuka Kofun == In 2005 (Heisei 17), a privately published booklet titled ''Now I Speak Out: The Horned Helmet Excavated from Ōzuka'' by Sadawo Iwashita was released. Iwashita, originally from Toyotomi Village, recounted encountering the helmet in 1932 and facilitating its transfer to the Imperial Household Museum (now the Tokyo National Museum). [[File:Western areas Kofu Basin,from the Ouzuka-Kofun.jpg|thumb|left|500px|View of the northwestern Kōfu Basin from Ōzuka Kofun (Photographed in August 2013).]] {{-}} == References == * Hidenori Okano, "Ōzuka Kofun," ''Yamanashi Prefectural History: Archaeology Volume 1'' * Sadawo Iwashita, ''Now I Speak Out: The Horned Helmet Excavated from Ōzuka'', privately published, 2005. * Sukenobu Sueki, "On the Horned Helmet Excavated from Ōzuka Kofun," ''Journal of Yamanashi Archaeological Association, Issue 18,'' 2008. * ''Kai's Black Horse - Horses that shaped History,'' Yamanashi Prefectural Museum, 2014. ** Akihiro Morihara, "Did the Master of Kai-Chōshizuka See Horses?" == notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.chuo.yamanashi.jp/sougou/bunkazai.php?id=36 Yamanashi Prefecture Chūō City Comprehensive Guide: Ōzuka Kofun] * [http://www.manabi.pref.yamanashi.jp/db/servlet/dbview?id=A810000413 Yamanashi Navi Net designation1 Intypeation: Ōzuka Kofun] * [http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/kouko-hak/special/special-08haru.html Yamanashi Prefecture Archaeological Museum] (Exhibit on keyhole tombs from their emergence to disappearance) {{Japanese-history-stub}} * [[Isezuka Kofun (Chūō)]] ...Kofun]] ([[kofun]]) located in Takabe, {{ill|Chūō, Yamanashi|ja|中央市|12=draft}}, Yamanashi Prefecture. The tomb is classified as a [[circular burial moun ...gside nearby Kofuns like the {{ill|Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)|ja|王塚古墳 (中央市)|12=draft}}, it constitutes part of the Uyamadaira Kofun Group. {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Ōzuka Kofun | location = Ōtorii 4256, Chūō City, Yamanashi Prefecture[http://www.city.chuo.yamanashi.jp/sougou/gappei/data/meeting2_21.pdf Regarding the Handling of Social Education] - Tamaho Town, Tatomi Town, Toyotomi Village Merger Council | coordinates = {{Coord|35|34|46.6|N|138|33|15.1|E|map_size:20000_region:JP-19_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = Keyhole-shaped kofun | built = Early 5th century | area = Diameter: 40 m, Height: 7 m | burial = | burial goods = Iron axe | designation1 = City-Historic Site1 historical site | image = [[File:Ouzuka-Kofun Old tomb Chuo-City from southeastern side.JPG|280px]]
Ōzuka Kofun (Photographed in August 2013) }} [[File:Ouzuka-Kofun Old tomb Chuo-City from west side.JPG|thumb|280px|Ōzuka Kofun, view from the west (Photographed in August 2013)]] '''Ōzuka Kofun''' (王塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun ({{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}}) located in {{ill|Chūō City|ja|中央市|12=draft}}, Yamanashi Prefecture. Its type is debated as either a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} or a scallop-shaped kofun. == location and Historical Context == Ōtorii in Chūō City lies on the southern edge of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|12=draft}}, at the western end of the {{ill|Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵|12=draft}}, on a plateau called Uyamahira bordered by the Shichisato and Asari rivers. The site is 342 meters above sea level. This area hosts numerous archaeological sites from the {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代|12=draft}} through the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代|12=draft}} and {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft}}, typeing the Uyamahira Kofun Cluster with other mounds such as {{ill|Futagozuka Kofun|ja|二子塚古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Isezuka Kofun (Chūō City)|ja|伊勢塚古墳 (中央市)|12=draft}}. The mound is scallop-shaped but has also been evaluated as a keyhole-shaped kofun. It measures approximately 64 meters in length and 7 meters in height, and it is estimated to have been constructed in the late 5th century. == Excavations and burial goods == The posterior mound has been subject to looting, and later investigations confirmed a vertical pit-style stone chamber. The tomb was discovered in 1930 (Shōwa 5) and excavated by Yoshio Nishina. It was Historic Site1 a cultural property of {{ill|Toyotomi, Yamanashi|ja|豊富村 (山梨県)|12=draft}} in 1970 (Shōwa 45) and is now a city-Historic Site1 cultural property of Chūō City. Excavated burial goods include iron weapons such as swords, knives, and arrowheads, as well as armor, horse fittings, and cylindrical haniwa. The {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft}} houses a “{{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|12=draft}}” said to have been unearthed in Toyotomi Village. burial goods from the tomb include F-shaped bridles, with other horse fittings from the same era found at the {{ill|Kankan-zuka Kofun|ja|かんかん塚古墳|12=draft}} in the Nakatō Kofun Cluster, part of the Sone Hills, which are considered the oldest horse fittings in the Tokai region from the late 5th centuryMorihara (2014), p.27. Additional burial goods include elliptical bridle pieces from {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster (Ichikawa Misato Town)|ja|大塚古墳群 (市川三郷町)|12=draft}}. In Yamanashi Prefecture, horse teeth excavated at the {{ill|Shiobe Ruins|ja|塩部遺跡|12=draft}} in Kōfu City and at the {{ill|Higashiyama-kita Ruins|ja|東山北遺跡|12=draft}} suggest the arrival of horses in the late 4th century. The construction of small to mid-sized mounds in areas outside the Sone Hills from the late 4th century to the emergence of horse fittings in the 5th century reflects potential social transtypeationsMorihara (2014), p.28. == Significance of Ōzuka Kofun == Ōzuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 5th century based on its burial burial goods. Though not thoroughly investigated, its vertical pit-style stone chamber and scallop-shaped or keyhole-shaped type make it a notable example of ancient tombs in the region. Kofun construction in Yamanashi Prefecture began in the mid-4th century with large tombs like the {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|12=draft}} in the Sone Hills and shifted to smaller-map_size tombs throughout the Kōfu Basin by the mid-to-late 5th century. Ōzuka Kofun is part of this transitional phase, contemporaneous with other mounds such as {{ill|Kitsunezuka Kofun|ja|狐塚古墳|12=draft}} in Fuefuki City and {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun (disambiguation)|ja|大塚古墳|12=draft}} in Ichikawa Misato Town. This period was marked by increased militarization across Japan, driven by geopolitical tensions on the Korean Peninsula and the eastward expansion of the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト王権|12=draft}}. Tombs in Yamanashi during this era began to feature military-related burial goods such as armor. == Legacy of the “Horned Helmet” Attributed to Ōzuka Kofun == In 2005 (Heisei 17), a privately published booklet titled ''Now I Speak Out: The Horned Helmet Excavated from Ōzuka'' by Sadawo Iwashita was released. Iwashita, originally from Toyotomi Village, recounted encountering the helmet in 1932 and facilitating its transfer to the Imperial Household Museum (now the Tokyo National Museum). [[File:Western areas Kofu Basin,from the Ouzuka-Kofun.jpg|thumb|left|500px|View of the northwestern Kōfu Basin from Ōzuka Kofun (Photographed in August 2013).]] {{-}} == References == * Hidenori Okano, "Ōzuka Kofun," ''Yamanashi Prefectural History: Archaeology Volume 1'' * Sadawo Iwashita, ''Now I Speak Out: The Horned Helmet Excavated from Ōzuka'', privately published, 2005. * Sukenobu Sueki, "On the Horned Helmet Excavated from Ōzuka Kofun," ''Journal of Yamanashi Archaeological Association, Issue 18,'' 2008. * ''Kai's Black Horse - Horses that shaped History,'' Yamanashi Prefectural Museum, 2014. ** Akihiro Morihara, "Did the Master of Kai-Chōshizuka See Horses?" == notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.chuo.yamanashi.jp/sougou/bunkazai.php?id=36 Yamanashi Prefecture Chūō City Comprehensive Guide: Ōzuka Kofun] * [http://www.manabi.pref.yamanashi.jp/db/servlet/dbview?id=A810000413 Yamanashi Navi Net designation1 Intypeation: Ōzuka Kofun] * [http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/kouko-hak/special/special-08haru.html Yamanashi Prefecture Archaeological Museum] (Exhibit on keyhole tombs from their emergence to disappearance) {{Japanese-history-stub}} * [[Isezuka Kofun (Chūō)]] ...Kofun]] ([[kofun]]) located in Takabe, {{ill|Chūō, Yamanashi|ja|中央市|12=draft}}, Yamanashi Prefecture. The tomb is classified as a [[circular burial moun ...gside nearby Kofuns like the {{ill|Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)|ja|王塚古墳 (中央市)|12=draft}}, it constitutes part of the Uyamadaira Kofun Group. The name "Isetsuka" ... 5 KB (599 words) - 22:23, 14 January [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:History of Chūō City]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:City-Historic Site1 historical sites in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:王塚古墳 (中央市)]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:to be resorted]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] [[Category:qq]]{{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)]] [[ja:王塚古墳 (中央市)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)}}

Though not thoroughly investigated, its vertical pit-style stone chamber and scallop-shaped or keyhole-shaped type make it a notable example of ancient tombs in the region. Kofun construction in Yamanashi Prefecture began in the mid-4th century with large tombs like the {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|12=draft}} in the Sone Hills and shifted to smaller-map_size tombs throughout the Kōfu Basin by the mid-to-late 5th century. Ōzuka Kofun is part of this transitional phase, contemporaneous with other mounds such as {{ill|Kitsunezuka Kofun|ja|狐塚古墳|12=draft}} in Fuefuki City and {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun (disambiguation)|ja|大塚古墳|12=draft}} in Ichikawa Misato Town. This period was marked by increased militarization across Japan, driven by geopolitical tensions on the Korean Peninsula and the eastward expansion of the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト王権|12=draft}}. Tombs in Yamanashi during this era began to feature military-related burial goods such as armor. == Legacy of the “Horned Helmet” Attributed to Ōzuka Kofun == In 2005 (Heisei 17), a privately published booklet titled ''Now I Speak Out: The Horned Helmet Excavated from Ōzuka'' by Sadawo Iwashita was released. Iwashita, originally from Toyotomi Village, recounted encountering the helmet in 1932 and facilitating its transfer to the Imperial Household Museum (now the Tokyo National Museum). [[File:Western areas Kofu Basin,from the Ouzuka-Kofun.jpg|thumb|left|500px|View of the northwestern Kōfu Basin from Ōzuka Kofun (Photographed in August 2013).]] {{-}} == References == * Hidenori Okano, "Ōzuka Kofun," ''Yamanashi Prefectural History: Archaeology Volume 1'' * Sadawo Iwashita, ''Now I Speak Out: The Horned Helmet Excavated from Ōzuka'', privately published, 2005. * Sukenobu Sueki, "On the Horned Helmet Excavated from Ōzuka Kofun," ''Journal of Yamanashi Archaeological Association, Issue 18,'' 2008. * ''Kai's Black Horse - Horses that shaped History,'' Yamanashi Prefectural Museum, 2014. ** Akihiro Morihara, "Did the Master of Kai-Chōshizuka See Horses?" == notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.chuo.yamanashi.jp/sougou/bunkazai.php?id=36 Yamanashi Prefecture Chūō City Comprehensive Guide: Ōzuka Kofun] * [http://www.manabi.pref.yamanashi.jp/db/servlet/dbview?id=A810000413 Yamanashi Navi Net designation1 Intypeation: Ōzuka Kofun] * [http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/kouko-hak/special/special-08haru.html Yamanashi Prefecture Archaeological Museum] (Exhibit on keyhole tombs from their emergence to disappearance) {{Japanese-history-stub}} * [[Isezuka Kofun (Chūō)]] ...Kofun]] ([[kofun]]) located in Takabe, {{ill|Chūō, Yamanashi|ja|中央市|12=draft}}, Yamanashi Prefecture. The tomb is classified as a [[circular burial moun ...gside nearby Kofuns like the {{ill|Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)|ja|王塚古墳 (中央市)|12=draft}}, it constitutes part of the Uyamadaira Kofun Group. The name "Isetsuka" ... 5 KB (599 words) - 22:23, 14 January [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:History of Chūō City]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:City-Historic Site1 historical sites in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:王塚古墳 (中央市)]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:to be resorted]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)]] [[ja:王塚古墳 (中央市)]] {{citation needed|Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)}} {{Kofun navbar}}

revision.comment: Moving from [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] to [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:{{subst:PAGENAME}}[[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Ōzuka Kofun | location = Ōtorii 4256, Chūō City, Yamanashi Prefecture[http://www.city.chuo.yamanashi.jp/sougou/gappei/data/meeting2_21.pdf Regarding the Handling of Social Education] - Tamaho Town, Tatomi Town, Toyotomi Village Merger Council | coordinates = {{Coord|35|34|46.6|N|138|33|15.1|E|map_size:20000_region:JP-19_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = Keyhole-shaped kofun | built = Early 5th century | area = Diameter: 40 m, Height: 7 m | burial = | burial goods = Iron axe | designation1 = City-Historic Site1 historical site | image = [[File:Ouzuka-Kofun Old tomb Chuo-City from southeastern side.JPG|280px]]
Ōzuka Kofun (Photographed in August 2013) }} [[File:Ouzuka-Kofun Old tomb Chuo-City from west side.JPG|thumb|280px|Ōzuka Kofun, view from the west (Photographed in August 2013)]] '''Ōzuka Kofun''' (王塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun ({{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}}) located in {{ill|Chūō City|ja|中央市|12=draft}}, Yamanashi Prefecture. Its type is debated as either a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} or a scallop-shaped kofun. == location and Historical Context == Ōtorii in Chūō City lies on the southern edge of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|12=draft}}, at the western end of the {{ill|Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵|12=draft}}, on a plateau called Uyamahira bordered by the Shichisato and Asari rivers. The site is 342 meters above sea level. This area hosts numerous archaeological sites from the {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代|12=draft}} through the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代|12=draft}} and {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft}}, typeing the Uyamahira Kofun Cluster with other mounds such as {{ill|Futagozuka Kofun|ja|二子塚古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Isezuka Kofun (Chūō City)|ja|伊勢塚古墳 (中央市)|12=draft}}. The mound is scallop-shaped but has also been evaluated as a keyhole-shaped kofun. It measures approximately 64 meters in length and 7 meters in height, and it is estimated to have been constructed in the late 5th century. == Excavations and burial goods == The posterior mound has been subject to looting, and later investigations confirmed a vertical pit-style stone chamber. The tomb was discovered in 1930 (Shōwa 5) and excavated by Yoshio Nishina. It was Historic Site1 a cultural property of {{ill|Toyotomi, Yamanashi|ja|豊富村 (山梨県)|12=draft}} in 1970 (Shōwa 45) and is now a city-Historic Site1 cultural property of Chūō City. Excavated burial goods include iron weapons such as swords, knives, and arrowheads, as well as armor, horse fittings, and cylindrical haniwa. The {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft}} houses a “{{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|12=draft}}” said to have been unearthed in Toyotomi Village. burial goods from the tomb include F-shaped bridles, with other horse fittings from the same era found at the {{ill|Kankan-zuka Kofun|ja|かんかん塚古墳|12=draft}} in the Nakatō Kofun Cluster, part of the Sone Hills, which are considered the oldest horse fittings in the Tokai region from the late 5th centuryMorihara (2014), p.27. Additional burial goods include elliptical bridle pieces from {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster (Ichikawa Misato Town)|ja|大塚古墳群 (市川三郷町)|12=draft}}. In Yamanashi Prefecture, horse teeth excavated at the {{ill|Shiobe Ruins|ja|塩部遺跡|12=draft}} in Kōfu City and at the {{ill|Higashiyama-kita Ruins|ja|東山北遺跡|12=draft}} suggest the arrival of horses in the late 4th century. The construction of small to mid-sized mounds in areas outside the Sone Hills from the late 4th century to the emergence of horse fittings in the 5th century reflects potential social transtypeationsMorihara (2014), p.28. == Significance of Ōzuka Kofun == Ōzuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 5th century based on its burial burial goods. Though not thoroughly investigated, its vertical pit-style stone chamber and scallop-shaped or keyhole-shaped type make it a notable example of ancient tombs in the region. Kofun construction in Yamanashi Prefecture began in the mid-4th century with large tombs like the {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|12=draft}} in the Sone Hills and shifted to smaller-map_size tombs throughout the Kōfu Basin by the mid-to-late 5th century. Ōzuka Kofun is part of this transitional phase, contemporaneous with other mounds such as {{ill|Kitsunezuka Kofun|ja|狐塚古墳|12=draft}} in Fuefuki City and {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun (disambiguation)|ja|大塚古墳|12=draft}} in Ichikawa Misato Town. This period was marked by increased militarization across Japan, driven by geopolitical tensions on the Korean Peninsula and the eastward expansion of the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト王権|12=draft}}. Tombs in Yamanashi during this era began to feature military-related burial goods such as armor. == Legacy of the “Horned Helmet” Attributed to Ōzuka Kofun == In 2005 (Heisei 17), a privately published booklet titled ''Now I Speak Out: The Horned Helmet Excavated from Ōzuka'' by Sadawo Iwashita was released. Iwashita, originally from Toyotomi Village, recounted encountering the helmet in 1932 and facilitating its transfer to the Imperial Household Museum (now the Tokyo National Museum). [[File:Western areas Kofu Basin,from the Ouzuka-Kofun.jpg|thumb|left|500px|View of the northwestern Kōfu Basin from Ōzuka Kofun (Photographed in August 2013).]] {{-}} == References == * Hidenori Okano, "Ōzuka Kofun," ''Yamanashi Prefectural History: Archaeology Volume 1'' * Sadawo Iwashita, ''Now I Speak Out: The Horned Helmet Excavated from Ōzuka'', privately published, 2005. * Sukenobu Sueki, "On the Horned Helmet Excavated from Ōzuka Kofun," ''Journal of Yamanashi Archaeological Association, Issue 18,'' 2008. * ''Kai's Black Horse - Horses that shaped History,'' Yamanashi Prefectural Museum, 2014. ** Akihiro Morihara, "Did the Master of Kai-Chōshizuka See Horses?" == notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.chuo.yamanashi.jp/sougou/bunkazai.php?id=36 Yamanashi Prefecture Chūō City Comprehensive Guide: Ōzuka Kofun] * [http://www.manabi.pref.yamanashi.jp/db/servlet/dbview?id=A810000413 Yamanashi Navi Net designation1 Intypeation: Ōzuka Kofun] * [http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/kouko-hak/special/special-08haru.html Yamanashi Prefecture Archaeological Museum] (Exhibit on keyhole tombs from their emergence to disappearance) {{Japanese-history-stub}} * [[Isezuka Kofun (Chūō)]] ...Kofun]] ([[kofun]]) located in Takabe, {{ill|Chūō, Yamanashi|ja|中央市|12=draft}}, Yamanashi Prefecture. The tomb is classified as a [[circular burial moun ...gside nearby Kofuns like the {{ill|Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)|ja|王塚古墳 (中央市)|12=draft}}, it constitutes part of the Uyamadaira Kofun Group. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Ōzuka Kofun | location = Ōtorii 4256, Chūō City, Yamanashi Prefecture[http://www.city.chuo.yamanashi.jp/sougou/gappei/data/meeting2_21.pdf Regarding the Handling of Social Education] - Tamaho Town, Tatomi Town, Toyotomi Village Merger Council | coordinates = {{Coord|35|34|46.6|N|138|33|15.1|E|map_size:20000_region:JP-19_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = Keyhole-shaped kofun | built = Early 5th century | area = Diameter: 40 m, Height: 7 m | burial = | burial goods = Iron axe | designation1 = City-Historic Site1 historical site | image = [[File:Ouzuka-Kofun Old tomb Chuo-City from southeastern side.JPG|280px]]
Ōzuka Kofun (Photographed in August 2013) }} [[File:Ouzuka-Kofun Old tomb Chuo-City from west side.JPG|thumb|280px|Ōzuka Kofun, view from the west (Photographed in August 2013)]] '''Ōzuka Kofun''' (王塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun ({{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}}) located in {{ill|Chūō City|ja|中央市|12=draft}}, Yamanashi Prefecture. Its type is debated as either a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} or a scallop-shaped kofun. == location and Historical Context == Ōtorii in Chūō City lies on the southern edge of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|12=draft}}, at the western end of the {{ill|Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵|12=draft}}, on a plateau called Uyamahira bordered by the Shichisato and Asari rivers. The site is 342 meters above sea level. This area hosts numerous archaeological sites from the {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代|12=draft}} through the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代|12=draft}} and {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft}}, typeing the Uyamahira Kofun Cluster with other mounds such as {{ill|Futagozuka Kofun|ja|二子塚古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Isezuka Kofun (Chūō City)|ja|伊勢塚古墳 (中央市)|12=draft}}. The mound is scallop-shaped but has also been evaluated as a keyhole-shaped kofun. It measures approximately 64 meters in length and 7 meters in height, and it is estimated to have been constructed in the late 5th century. == Excavations and burial goods == The posterior mound has been subject to looting, and later investigations confirmed a vertical pit-style stone chamber. The tomb was discovered in 1930 (Shōwa 5) and excavated by Yoshio Nishina. It was Historic Site1 a cultural property of {{ill|Toyotomi, Yamanashi|ja|豊富村 (山梨県)|12=draft}} in 1970 (Shōwa 45) and is now a city-Historic Site1 cultural property of Chūō City. Excavated burial goods include iron weapons such as swords, knives, and arrowheads, as well as armor, horse fittings, and cylindrical haniwa. The {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft}} houses a “{{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|12=draft}}” said to have been unearthed in Toyotomi Village. burial goods from the tomb include F-shaped bridles, with other horse fittings from the same era found at the {{ill|Kankan-zuka Kofun|ja|かんかん塚古墳|12=draft}} in the Nakatō Kofun Cluster, part of the Sone Hills, which are considered the oldest horse fittings in the Tokai region from the late 5th centuryMorihara (2014), p.27. Additional burial goods include elliptical bridle pieces from {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster (Ichikawa Misato Town)|ja|大塚古墳群 (市川三郷町)|12=draft}}. In Yamanashi Prefecture, horse teeth excavated at the {{ill|Shiobe Ruins|ja|塩部遺跡|12=draft}} in Kōfu City and at the {{ill|Higashiyama-kita Ruins|ja|東山北遺跡|12=draft}} suggest the arrival of horses in the late 4th century. The construction of small to mid-sized mounds in areas outside the Sone Hills from the late 4th century to the emergence of horse fittings in the 5th century reflects potential social transtypeationsMorihara (2014), p.28. == Significance of Ōzuka Kofun == Ōzuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 5th century based on its burial burial goods. Though not thoroughly investigated, its vertical pit-style stone chamber and scallop-shaped or keyhole-shaped type make it a notable example of ancient tombs in the region. Kofun construction in Yamanashi Prefecture began in the mid-4th century with large tombs like the {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|12=draft}} in the Sone Hills and shifted to smaller-map_size tombs throughout the Kōfu Basin by the mid-to-late 5th century. Ōzuka Kofun is part of this transitional phase, contemporaneous with other mounds such as {{ill|Kitsunezuka Kofun|ja|狐塚古墳|12=draft}} in Fuefuki City and {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun (disambiguation)|ja|大塚古墳|12=draft}} in Ichikawa Misato Town. This period was marked by increased militarization across Japan, driven by geopolitical tensions on the Korean Peninsula and the eastward expansion of the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト王権|12=draft}}. Tombs in Yamanashi during this era began to feature military-related burial goods such as armor. == Legacy of the “Horned Helmet” Attributed to Ōzuka Kofun == In 2005 (Heisei 17), a privately published booklet titled ''Now I Speak Out: The Horned Helmet Excavated from Ōzuka'' by Sadawo Iwashita was released. Iwashita, originally from Toyotomi Village, recounted encountering the helmet in 1932 and facilitating its transfer to the Imperial Household Museum (now the Tokyo National Museum). [[File:Western areas Kofu Basin,from the Ouzuka-Kofun.jpg|thumb|left|500px|View of the northwestern Kōfu Basin from Ōzuka Kofun (Photographed in August 2013).]] {{-}} == References == * Hidenori Okano, "Ōzuka Kofun," ''Yamanashi Prefectural History: Archaeology Volume 1'' * Sadawo Iwashita, ''Now I Speak Out: The Horned Helmet Excavated from Ōzuka'', privately published, 2005. * Sukenobu Sueki, "On the Horned Helmet Excavated from Ōzuka Kofun," ''Journal of Yamanashi Archaeological Association, Issue 18,'' 2008. * ''Kai's Black Horse - Horses that shaped History,'' Yamanashi Prefectural Museum, 2014. ** Akihiro Morihara, "Did the Master of Kai-Chōshizuka See Horses?" == notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.chuo.yamanashi.jp/sougou/bunkazai.php?id=36 Yamanashi Prefecture Chūō City Comprehensive Guide: Ōzuka Kofun] * [http://www.manabi.pref.yamanashi.jp/db/servlet/dbview?id=A810000413 Yamanashi Navi Net designation1 Intypeation: Ōzuka Kofun] * [http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/kouko-hak/special/special-08haru.html Yamanashi Prefecture Archaeological Museum] (Exhibit on keyhole tombs from their emergence to disappearance) {{Japanese-history-stub}} * [[Isezuka Kofun (Chūō)]] ...Kofun]] ([[kofun]]) located in Takabe, {{ill|Chūō, Yamanashi|ja|中央市|12=draft}}, Yamanashi Prefecture. The tomb is classified as a [[circular burial moun ...gside nearby Kofuns like the {{ill|Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)|ja|王塚古墳 (中央市)|12=draft}}, it constitutes part of the Uyamadaira Kofun Group. The name "Isetsuka" ... 5 KB (599 words) - 22:23, 14 January [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:History of Chūō City]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:City-Historic Site1 historical sites in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:王塚古墳 (中央市)]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:to be resorted]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)]] [[ja:王塚古墳 (中央市)]] {{citation needed|Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)}} {{Kofun navbar}}

Iwashita, originally from Toyotomi Village, recounted encountering the helmet in 1932 and facilitating its transfer to the Imperial Household Museum (now the Tokyo National Museum). [[File:Western areas Kofu Basin,from the Ouzuka-Kofun.jpg|thumb|left|500px|View of the northwestern Kōfu Basin from Ōzuka Kofun (Photographed in August 2013).]] {{-}} == References == * Hidenori Okano, "Ōzuka Kofun," ''Yamanashi Prefectural History: Archaeology Volume 1'' * Sadawo Iwashita, ''Now I Speak Out: The Horned Helmet Excavated from Ōzuka'', privately published, 2005. * Sukenobu Sueki, "On the Horned Helmet Excavated from Ōzuka Kofun," ''Journal of Yamanashi Archaeological Association, Issue 18,'' 2008. * ''Kai's Black Horse - Horses that shaped History,'' Yamanashi Prefectural Museum, 2014. ** Akihiro Morihara, "Did the Master of Kai-Chōshizuka See Horses?" == notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.chuo.yamanashi.jp/sougou/bunkazai.php?id=36 Yamanashi Prefecture Chūō City Comprehensive Guide: Ōzuka Kofun] * [http://www.manabi.pref.yamanashi.jp/db/servlet/dbview?id=A810000413 Yamanashi Navi Net designation1 Intypeation: Ōzuka Kofun] * [http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/kouko-hak/special/special-08haru.html Yamanashi Prefecture Archaeological Museum] (Exhibit on keyhole tombs from their emergence to disappearance) {{Japanese-history-stub}} * [[Isezuka Kofun (Chūō)]] ...Kofun]] ([[kofun]]) located in Takabe, {{ill|Chūō, Yamanashi|ja|中央市|12=draft}}, Yamanashi Prefecture. The tomb is classified as a [[circular burial moun ...gside nearby Kofuns like the {{ill|Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)|ja|王塚古墳 (中央市)|12=draft}}, it constitutes part of the Uyamadaira Kofun Group. {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Ōzuka Kofun | location = Ōtorii 4256, Chūō City, Yamanashi Prefecture[http://www.city.chuo.yamanashi.jp/sougou/gappei/data/meeting2_21.pdf Regarding the Handling of Social Education] - Tamaho Town, Tatomi Town, Toyotomi Village Merger Council | coordinates = {{Coord|35|34|46.6|N|138|33|15.1|E|map_size:20000_region:JP-19_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = Keyhole-shaped kofun | built = Early 5th century | area = Diameter: 40 m, Height: 7 m | burial = | burial goods = Iron axe | designation1 = City-Historic Site1 historical site | image = [[File:Ouzuka-Kofun Old tomb Chuo-City from southeastern side.JPG|280px]]
Ōzuka Kofun (Photographed in August 2013) }} [[File:Ouzuka-Kofun Old tomb Chuo-City from west side.JPG|thumb|280px|Ōzuka Kofun, view from the west (Photographed in August 2013)]] '''Ōzuka Kofun''' (王塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun ({{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}}) located in {{ill|Chūō City|ja|中央市|12=draft}}, Yamanashi Prefecture. Its type is debated as either a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} or a scallop-shaped kofun. == location and Historical Context == Ōtorii in Chūō City lies on the southern edge of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|12=draft}}, at the western end of the {{ill|Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵|12=draft}}, on a plateau called Uyamahira bordered by the Shichisato and Asari rivers. The site is 342 meters above sea level. This area hosts numerous archaeological sites from the {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代|12=draft}} through the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代|12=draft}} and {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft}}, typeing the Uyamahira Kofun Cluster with other mounds such as {{ill|Futagozuka Kofun|ja|二子塚古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Isezuka Kofun (Chūō City)|ja|伊勢塚古墳 (中央市)|12=draft}}. The mound is scallop-shaped but has also been evaluated as a keyhole-shaped kofun. It measures approximately 64 meters in length and 7 meters in height, and it is estimated to have been constructed in the late 5th century. == Excavations and burial goods == The posterior mound has been subject to looting, and later investigations confirmed a vertical pit-style stone chamber. The tomb was discovered in 1930 (Shōwa 5) and excavated by Yoshio Nishina. It was Historic Site1 a cultural property of {{ill|Toyotomi, Yamanashi|ja|豊富村 (山梨県)|12=draft}} in 1970 (Shōwa 45) and is now a city-Historic Site1 cultural property of Chūō City. Excavated burial goods include iron weapons such as swords, knives, and arrowheads, as well as armor, horse fittings, and cylindrical haniwa. The {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft}} houses a “{{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|12=draft}}” said to have been unearthed in Toyotomi Village. burial goods from the tomb include F-shaped bridles, with other horse fittings from the same era found at the {{ill|Kankan-zuka Kofun|ja|かんかん塚古墳|12=draft}} in the Nakatō Kofun Cluster, part of the Sone Hills, which are considered the oldest horse fittings in the Tokai region from the late 5th centuryMorihara (2014), p.27. Additional burial goods include elliptical bridle pieces from {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster (Ichikawa Misato Town)|ja|大塚古墳群 (市川三郷町)|12=draft}}. In Yamanashi Prefecture, horse teeth excavated at the {{ill|Shiobe Ruins|ja|塩部遺跡|12=draft}} in Kōfu City and at the {{ill|Higashiyama-kita Ruins|ja|東山北遺跡|12=draft}} suggest the arrival of horses in the late 4th century. The construction of small to mid-sized mounds in areas outside the Sone Hills from the late 4th century to the emergence of horse fittings in the 5th century reflects potential social transtypeationsMorihara (2014), p.28. == Significance of Ōzuka Kofun == Ōzuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 5th century based on its burial burial goods. Though not thoroughly investigated, its vertical pit-style stone chamber and scallop-shaped or keyhole-shaped type make it a notable example of ancient tombs in the region. Kofun construction in Yamanashi Prefecture began in the mid-4th century with large tombs like the {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|12=draft}} in the Sone Hills and shifted to smaller-map_size tombs throughout the Kōfu Basin by the mid-to-late 5th century. Ōzuka Kofun is part of this transitional phase, contemporaneous with other mounds such as {{ill|Kitsunezuka Kofun|ja|狐塚古墳|12=draft}} in Fuefuki City and {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun (disambiguation)|ja|大塚古墳|12=draft}} in Ichikawa Misato Town. This period was marked by increased militarization across Japan, driven by geopolitical tensions on the Korean Peninsula and the eastward expansion of the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト王権|12=draft}}. Tombs in Yamanashi during this era began to feature military-related burial goods such as armor. == Legacy of the “Horned Helmet” Attributed to Ōzuka Kofun == In 2005 (Heisei 17), a privately published booklet titled ''Now I Speak Out: The Horned Helmet Excavated from Ōzuka'' by Sadawo Iwashita was released. Iwashita, originally from Toyotomi Village, recounted encountering the helmet in 1932 and facilitating its transfer to the Imperial Household Museum (now the Tokyo National Museum). [[File:Western areas Kofu Basin,from the Ouzuka-Kofun.jpg|thumb|left|500px|View of the northwestern Kōfu Basin from Ōzuka Kofun (Photographed in August 2013).]] {{-}} == References == * Hidenori Okano, "Ōzuka Kofun," ''Yamanashi Prefectural History: Archaeology Volume 1'' * Sadawo Iwashita, ''Now I Speak Out: The Horned Helmet Excavated from Ōzuka'', privately published, 2005. * Sukenobu Sueki, "On the Horned Helmet Excavated from Ōzuka Kofun," ''Journal of Yamanashi Archaeological Association, Issue 18,'' 2008. * ''Kai's Black Horse - Horses that shaped History,'' Yamanashi Prefectural Museum, 2014. ** Akihiro Morihara, "Did the Master of Kai-Chōshizuka See Horses?" == notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.chuo.yamanashi.jp/sougou/bunkazai.php?id=36 Yamanashi Prefecture Chūō City Comprehensive Guide: Ōzuka Kofun] * [http://www.manabi.pref.yamanashi.jp/db/servlet/dbview?id=A810000413 Yamanashi Navi Net designation1 Intypeation: Ōzuka Kofun] * [http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/kouko-hak/special/special-08haru.html Yamanashi Prefecture Archaeological Museum] (Exhibit on keyhole tombs from their emergence to disappearance) {{Japanese-history-stub}} * [[Isezuka Kofun (Chūō)]] ...Kofun]] ([[kofun]]) located in Takabe, {{ill|Chūō, Yamanashi|ja|中央市|12=draft}}, Yamanashi Prefecture. The tomb is classified as a [[circular burial moun ...gside nearby Kofuns like the {{ill|Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)|ja|王塚古墳 (中央市)|12=draft}}, it constitutes part of the Uyamadaira Kofun Group. The name "Isetsuka" ... 5 KB (599 words) - 22:23, 14 January [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:History of Chūō City]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:City-Historic Site1 historical sites in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:王塚古墳 (中央市)]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:to be resorted]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)]] [[ja:王塚古墳 (中央市)]] {{citation needed|Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)}} {{Kofun navbar}}

Ōzuka Kofun (Photographed in August 2013) }} [[File:Ouzuka-Kofun Old tomb Chuo-City from west side.JPG|thumb|280px|Ōzuka Kofun, view from the west (Photographed in August 2013)]] '''Ōzuka Kofun''' (王塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun ({{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}}) located in {{ill|Chūō City|ja|中央市|12=draft}}, Yamanashi Prefecture. Its type is debated as either a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} or a scallop-shaped kofun. == location and Historical Context == Ōtorii in Chūō City lies on the southern edge of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|12=draft}}, at the western end of the {{ill|Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵|12=draft}}, on a plateau called Uyamahira bordered by the Shichisato and Asari rivers. The site is 342 meters above sea level. This area hosts numerous archaeological sites from the {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代|12=draft}} through the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代|12=draft}} and {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft}}, typeing the Uyamahira Kofun Cluster with other mounds such as {{ill|Futagozuka Kofun|ja|二子塚古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Isezuka Kofun (Chūō City)|ja|伊勢塚古墳 (中央市)|12=draft}}. The mound is scallop-shaped but has also been evaluated as a keyhole-shaped kofun. It measures approximately 64 meters in length and 7 meters in height, and it is estimated to have been constructed in the late 5th century. == Excavations and burial goods == The posterior mound has been subject to looting, and later investigations confirmed a vertical pit-style stone chamber. The tomb was discovered in 1930 (Shōwa 5) and excavated by Yoshio Nishina. It was Historic Site1 a cultural property of {{ill|Toyotomi, Yamanashi|ja|豊富村 (山梨県)|12=draft}} in 1970 (Shōwa 45) and is now a city-Historic Site1 cultural property of Chūō City. Excavated burial goods include iron weapons such as swords, knives, and arrowheads, as well as armor, horse fittings, and cylindrical haniwa. The {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft}} houses a “{{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|12=draft}}” said to have been unearthed in Toyotomi Village. burial goods from the tomb include F-shaped bridles, with other horse fittings from the same era found at the {{ill|Kankan-zuka Kofun|ja|かんかん塚古墳|12=draft}} in the Nakatō Kofun Cluster, part of the Sone Hills, which are considered the oldest horse fittings in the Tokai region from the late 5th centuryMorihara (2014), p.27. Additional burial goods include elliptical bridle pieces from {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster (Ichikawa Misato Town)|ja|大塚古墳群 (市川三郷町)|12=draft}}. In Yamanashi Prefecture, horse teeth excavated at the {{ill|Shiobe Ruins|ja|塩部遺跡|12=draft}} in Kōfu City and at the {{ill|Higashiyama-kita Ruins|ja|東山北遺跡|12=draft}} suggest the arrival of horses in the late 4th century. The construction of small to mid-sized mounds in areas outside the Sone Hills from the late 4th century to the emergence of horse fittings in the 5th century reflects potential social transtypeationsMorihara (2014), p.28. == Significance of Ōzuka Kofun == Ōzuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 5th century based on its burial burial goods. Though not thoroughly investigated, its vertical pit-style stone chamber and scallop-shaped or keyhole-shaped type make it a notable example of ancient tombs in the region. Kofun construction in Yamanashi Prefecture began in the mid-4th century with large tombs like the {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|12=draft}} in the Sone Hills and shifted to smaller-map_size tombs throughout the Kōfu Basin by the mid-to-late 5th century. Ōzuka Kofun is part of this transitional phase, contemporaneous with other mounds such as {{ill|Kitsunezuka Kofun|ja|狐塚古墳|12=draft}} in Fuefuki City and {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun (disambiguation)|ja|大塚古墳|12=draft}} in Ichikawa Misato Town. This period was marked by increased militarization across Japan, driven by geopolitical tensions on the Korean Peninsula and the eastward expansion of the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト王権|12=draft}}. Tombs in Yamanashi during this era began to feature military-related burial goods such as armor. == Legacy of the “Horned Helmet” Attributed to Ōzuka Kofun == In 2005 (Heisei 17), a privately published booklet titled ''Now I Speak Out: The Horned Helmet Excavated from Ōzuka'' by Sadawo Iwashita was released. Iwashita, originally from Toyotomi Village, recounted encountering the helmet in 1932 and facilitating its transfer to the Imperial Household Museum (now the Tokyo National Museum). [[File:Western areas Kofu Basin,from the Ouzuka-Kofun.jpg|thumb|left|500px|View of the northwestern Kōfu Basin from Ōzuka Kofun (Photographed in August 2013).]] {{-}} == References == * Hidenori Okano, "Ōzuka Kofun," ''Yamanashi Prefectural History: Archaeology Volume 1'' * Sadawo Iwashita, ''Now I Speak Out: The Horned Helmet Excavated from Ōzuka'', privately published, 2005. * Sukenobu Sueki, "On the Horned Helmet Excavated from Ōzuka Kofun," ''Journal of Yamanashi Archaeological Association, Issue 18,'' 2008. * ''Kai's Black Horse - Horses that shaped History,'' Yamanashi Prefectural Museum, 2014. ** Akihiro Morihara, "Did the Master of Kai-Chōshizuka See Horses?" == notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.chuo.yamanashi.jp/sougou/bunkazai.php?id=36 Yamanashi Prefecture Chūō City Comprehensive Guide: Ōzuka Kofun] * [http://www.manabi.pref.yamanashi.jp/db/servlet/dbview?id=A810000413 Yamanashi Navi Net designation1 Intypeation: Ōzuka Kofun] * [http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/kouko-hak/special/special-08haru.html Yamanashi Prefecture Archaeological Museum] (Exhibit on keyhole tombs from their emergence to disappearance) {{Japanese-history-stub}} * [[Isezuka Kofun (Chūō)]] ...Kofun]] ([[kofun]]) located in Takabe, {{ill|Chūō, Yamanashi|ja|中央市|12=draft}}, Yamanashi Prefecture. The tomb is classified as a [[circular burial moun ...gside nearby Kofuns like the {{ill|Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)|ja|王塚古墳 (中央市)|12=draft}}, it constitutes part of the Uyamadaira Kofun Group. The name "Isetsuka" ... 5 KB (599 words) - 22:23, 14 January [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:History of Chūō City]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:City-Historic Site1 historical sites in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:王塚古墳 (中央市)]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:to be resorted]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)]] [[ja:王塚古墳 (中央市)]] {{citation needed|Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)}} {{Kofun navbar}}

Iwashita, originally from Toyotomi Village, recounted encountering the helmet in 1932 and facilitating its transfer to the Imperial Household Museum (now the Tokyo National Museum). [[File:Western areas Kofu Basin,from the Ouzuka-Kofun.jpg|thumb|left|500px|View of the northwestern Kōfu Basin from Ōzuka Kofun (Photographed in August 2013).]] {{-}} == References == * Hidenori Okano, "Ōzuka Kofun," ''Yamanashi Prefectural History: Archaeology Volume 1'' * Sadawo Iwashita, ''Now I Speak Out: The Horned Helmet Excavated from Ōzuka'', privately published, 2005. * Sukenobu Sueki, "On the Horned Helmet Excavated from Ōzuka Kofun," ''Journal of Yamanashi Archaeological Association, Issue 18,'' 2008. * ''Kai's Black Horse - Horses that shaped History,'' Yamanashi Prefectural Museum, 2014. ** Akihiro Morihara, "Did the Master of Kai-Chōshizuka See Horses?" == notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.chuo.yamanashi.jp/sougou/bunkazai.php?id=36 Yamanashi Prefecture Chūō City Comprehensive Guide: Ōzuka Kofun] * [http://www.manabi.pref.yamanashi.jp/db/servlet/dbview?id=A810000413 Yamanashi Navi Net designation1 Intypeation: Ōzuka Kofun] * [http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/kouko-hak/special/special-08haru.html Yamanashi Prefecture Archaeological Museum] (Exhibit on keyhole tombs from their emergence to disappearance) {{Japanese-history-stub}} * [[Isezuka Kofun (Chūō)]] ...Kofun]] ([[kofun]]) located in Takabe, {{ill|Chūō, Yamanashi|ja|中央市|12=draft}}, Yamanashi Prefecture. The tomb is classified as a [[circular burial moun ...gside nearby Kofuns like the {{ill|Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)|ja|王塚古墳 (中央市)|12=draft}}, it constitutes part of the Uyamadaira Kofun Group. {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Ōzuka Kofun | location = Ōtorii 4256, Chūō City, Yamanashi Prefecture[http://www.city.chuo.yamanashi.jp/sougou/gappei/data/meeting2_21.pdf Regarding the Handling of Social Education] - Tamaho Town, Tatomi Town, Toyotomi Village Merger Council | coordinates = {{Coord|35|34|46.6|N|138|33|15.1|E|map_size:20000_region:JP-19_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = Keyhole-shaped kofun | built = Early 5th century | area = Diameter: 40 m, Height: 7 m | burial = | burial goods = Iron axe | designation1 = City-Historic Site1 historical site | image = [[File:Ouzuka-Kofun Old tomb Chuo-City from southeastern side.JPG|280px]]
Ōzuka Kofun (Photographed in August 2013) }} [[File:Ouzuka-Kofun Old tomb Chuo-City from west side.JPG|thumb|280px|Ōzuka Kofun, view from the west (Photographed in August 2013)]] '''Ōzuka Kofun''' (王塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun ({{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}}) located in {{ill|Chūō City|ja|中央市|12=draft}}, Yamanashi Prefecture. Its type is debated as either a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} or a scallop-shaped kofun. == location and Historical Context == Ōtorii in Chūō City lies on the southern edge of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|12=draft}}, at the western end of the {{ill|Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵|12=draft}}, on a plateau called Uyamahira bordered by the Shichisato and Asari rivers. The site is 342 meters above sea level. This area hosts numerous archaeological sites from the {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代|12=draft}} through the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代|12=draft}} and {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft}}, typeing the Uyamahira Kofun Cluster with other mounds such as {{ill|Futagozuka Kofun|ja|二子塚古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Isezuka Kofun (Chūō City)|ja|伊勢塚古墳 (中央市)|12=draft}}. The mound is scallop-shaped but has also been evaluated as a keyhole-shaped kofun. It measures approximately 64 meters in length and 7 meters in height, and it is estimated to have been constructed in the late 5th century. == Excavations and burial goods == The posterior mound has been subject to looting, and later investigations confirmed a vertical pit-style stone chamber. The tomb was discovered in 1930 (Shōwa 5) and excavated by Yoshio Nishina. It was Historic Site1 a cultural property of {{ill|Toyotomi, Yamanashi|ja|豊富村 (山梨県)|12=draft}} in 1970 (Shōwa 45) and is now a city-Historic Site1 cultural property of Chūō City. Excavated burial goods include iron weapons such as swords, knives, and arrowheads, as well as armor, horse fittings, and cylindrical haniwa. The {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft}} houses a “{{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|12=draft}}” said to have been unearthed in Toyotomi Village. burial goods from the tomb include F-shaped bridles, with other horse fittings from the same era found at the {{ill|Kankan-zuka Kofun|ja|かんかん塚古墳|12=draft}} in the Nakatō Kofun Cluster, part of the Sone Hills, which are considered the oldest horse fittings in the Tokai region from the late 5th centuryMorihara (2014), p.27. Additional burial goods include elliptical bridle pieces from {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster (Ichikawa Misato Town)|ja|大塚古墳群 (市川三郷町)|12=draft}}. In Yamanashi Prefecture, horse teeth excavated at the {{ill|Shiobe Ruins|ja|塩部遺跡|12=draft}} in Kōfu City and at the {{ill|Higashiyama-kita Ruins|ja|東山北遺跡|12=draft}} suggest the arrival of horses in the late 4th century. The construction of small to mid-sized mounds in areas outside the Sone Hills from the late 4th century to the emergence of horse fittings in the 5th century reflects potential social transtypeationsMorihara (2014), p.28. == Significance of Ōzuka Kofun == Ōzuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 5th century based on its burial burial goods. Though not thoroughly investigated, its vertical pit-style stone chamber and scallop-shaped or keyhole-shaped type make it a notable example of ancient tombs in the region. Kofun construction in Yamanashi Prefecture began in the mid-4th century with large tombs like the {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|12=draft}} in the Sone Hills and shifted to smaller-map_size tombs throughout the Kōfu Basin by the mid-to-late 5th century. Ōzuka Kofun is part of this transitional phase, contemporaneous with other mounds such as {{ill|Kitsunezuka Kofun|ja|狐塚古墳|12=draft}} in Fuefuki City and {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun (disambiguation)|ja|大塚古墳|12=draft}} in Ichikawa Misato Town. This period was marked by increased militarization across Japan, driven by geopolitical tensions on the Korean Peninsula and the eastward expansion of the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト王権|12=draft}}. Tombs in Yamanashi during this era began to feature military-related burial goods such as armor. == Legacy of the “Horned Helmet” Attributed to Ōzuka Kofun == In 2005 (Heisei 17), a privately published booklet titled ''Now I Speak Out: The Horned Helmet Excavated from Ōzuka'' by Sadawo Iwashita was released. Iwashita, originally from Toyotomi Village, recounted encountering the helmet in 1932 and facilitating its transfer to the Imperial Household Museum (now the Tokyo National Museum). [[File:Western areas Kofu Basin,from the Ouzuka-Kofun.jpg|thumb|left|500px|View of the northwestern Kōfu Basin from Ōzuka Kofun (Photographed in August 2013).]] {{-}} == References == * Hidenori Okano, "Ōzuka Kofun," ''Yamanashi Prefectural History: Archaeology Volume 1'' * Sadawo Iwashita, ''Now I Speak Out: The Horned Helmet Excavated from Ōzuka'', privately published, 2005. * Sukenobu Sueki, "On the Horned Helmet Excavated from Ōzuka Kofun," ''Journal of Yamanashi Archaeological Association, Issue 18,'' 2008. * ''Kai's Black Horse - Horses that shaped History,'' Yamanashi Prefectural Museum, 2014. ** Akihiro Morihara, "Did the Master of Kai-Chōshizuka See Horses?" == notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.chuo.yamanashi.jp/sougou/bunkazai.php?id=36 Yamanashi Prefecture Chūō City Comprehensive Guide: Ōzuka Kofun] * [http://www.manabi.pref.yamanashi.jp/db/servlet/dbview?id=A810000413 Yamanashi Navi Net designation1 Intypeation: Ōzuka Kofun] * [http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/kouko-hak/special/special-08haru.html Yamanashi Prefecture Archaeological Museum] (Exhibit on keyhole tombs from their emergence to disappearance) {{Japanese-history-stub}} * [[Isezuka Kofun (Chūō)]] ...Kofun]] ([[kofun]]) located in Takabe, {{ill|Chūō, Yamanashi|ja|中央市|12=draft}}, Yamanashi Prefecture. The tomb is classified as a [[circular burial moun ...gside nearby Kofuns like the {{ill|Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)|ja|王塚古墳 (中央市)|12=draft}}, it constitutes part of the Uyamadaira Kofun Group. The name "Isetsuka" ... 5 KB (599 words) - 22:23, 14 January [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:History of Chūō City]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:City-Historic Site1 historical sites in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:王塚古墳 (中央市)]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)]] [[ja:王塚古墳 (中央市)]] {{citation needed|Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)}} {{Kofun navbar}}
