Ōzuka Kofun (Photographed in August 2013) }} [[File:Ouzuka-Kofun Old tomb Chuo-City from west side.JPG|thumb|280px|Ōzuka Kofun, view from the west (Photographed in August 2013)]] '''Ōzuka Kofun''' (王塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun ({{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}}) located in {{ill|Chūō City|ja|中央市|12=draft}}, Yamanashi Prefecture. Its type is debated as either a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} or a scallop-shaped kofun. == location and Historical Context == Ōtorii in Chūō City lies on the southern edge of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|12=draft}}, at the western end of the {{ill|Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵|12=draft}}, on a plateau called Uyamahira bordered by the Shichisato and Asari rivers. The site is 342 meters above sea level. This area hosts numerous archaeological sites from the {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代|12=draft}} through the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代|12=draft}} and {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft}}, typeing the Uyamahira Kofun Cluster with other mounds such as {{ill|Futagozuka Kofun|ja|二子塚古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Isezuka Kofun (Chūō City)|ja|伊勢塚古墳 (中央市)|12=draft}}. The mound is scallop-shaped but has also been evaluated as a keyhole-shaped kofun. It measures approximately 64 meters in length and 7 meters in height, and it is estimated to have been constructed in the late 5th century. == Excavations and burial goods == The posterior mound has been subject to looting, and later investigations confirmed a vertical pit-style stone chamber. The tomb was discovered in 1930 (Shōwa 5) and excavated by Yoshio Nishina. It was Historic Site1 a cultural property of {{ill|Toyotomi, Yamanashi|ja|豊富村 (山梨県)|12=draft}} in 1970 (Shōwa 45) and is now a city-Historic Site1 cultural property of Chūō City. Excavated burial goods include iron weapons such as swords, knives, and arrowheads, as well as armor, horse fittings, and cylindrical haniwa. The {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft}} houses a “{{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|12=draft}}” said to have been unearthed in Toyotomi Village. burial goods from the tomb include F-shaped bridles, with other horse fittings from the same era found at the {{ill|Kankan-zuka Kofun|ja|かんかん塚古墳|12=draft}} in the Nakatō Kofun Cluster, part of the Sone Hills, which are considered the oldest horse fittings in the Tokai region from the late 5th centuryMorihara (2014), p.27. Additional burial goods include elliptical bridle pieces from {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster (Ichikawa Misato Town)|ja|大塚古墳群 (市川三郷町)|12=draft}}. In Yamanashi Prefecture, horse teeth excavated at the {{ill|Shiobe Ruins|ja|塩部遺跡|12=draft}} in Kōfu City and at the {{ill|Higashiyama-kita Ruins|ja|東山北遺跡|12=draft}} suggest the arrival of horses in the late 4th century. The construction of small to mid-sized mounds in areas outside the Sone Hills from the late 4th century to the emergence of horse fittings in the 5th century reflects potential social transtypeationsMorihara (2014), p.28. == Significance of Ōzuka Kofun == Ōzuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 5th century based on its burial burial goods. Though not thoroughly investigated, its vertical pit-style stone chamber and scallop-shaped or keyhole-shaped type make it a notable example of ancient tombs in the region. Kofun construction in Yamanashi Prefecture began in the mid-4th century with large tombs like the {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|12=draft}} in the Sone Hills and shifted to smaller-map_size tombs throughout the Kōfu Basin by the mid-to-late 5th century. Ōzuka Kofun is part of this transitional phase, contemporaneous with other mounds such as {{ill|Kitsunezuka Kofun|ja|狐塚古墳|12=draft}} in Fuefuki City and {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun (disambiguation)|ja|大塚古墳|12=draft}} in Ichikawa Misato Town. This period was marked by increased militarization across Japan, driven by geopolitical tensions on the Korean Peninsula and the eastward expansion of the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト王権|12=draft}}. Tombs in Yamanashi during this era began to feature military-related burial goods such as armor. == Legacy of the “Horned Helmet” Attributed to Ōzuka Kofun == In 2005 (Heisei 17), a privately published booklet titled ''Now I Speak Out: The Horned Helmet Excavated from Ōzuka'' by Sadawo Iwashita was released. Iwashita, originally from Toyotomi Village, recounted encountering the helmet in 1932 and facilitating its transfer to the Imperial Household Museum (now the Tokyo National Museum). [[File:Western areas Kofu Basin,from the Ouzuka-Kofun.jpg|thumb|left|500px|View of the northwestern Kōfu Basin from Ōzuka Kofun (Photographed in August 2013).]] {{-}} == References == * Hidenori Okano, "Ōzuka Kofun," ''Yamanashi Prefectural History: Archaeology Volume 1'' * Sadawo Iwashita, ''Now I Speak Out: The Horned Helmet Excavated from Ōzuka'', privately published, 2005. * Sukenobu Sueki, "On the Horned Helmet Excavated from Ōzuka Kofun," ''Journal of Yamanashi Archaeological Association, Issue 18,'' 2008. * ''Kai's Black Horse - Horses that shaped History,'' Yamanashi Prefectural Museum, 2014. ** Akihiro Morihara, "Did the Master of Kai-Chōshizuka See Horses?" == notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.chuo.yamanashi.jp/sougou/bunkazai.php?id=36 Yamanashi Prefecture Chūō City Comprehensive Guide: Ōzuka Kofun] * [http://www.manabi.pref.yamanashi.jp/db/servlet/dbview?id=A810000413 Yamanashi Navi Net designation1 Intypeation: Ōzuka Kofun] * [http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/kouko-hak/special/special-08haru.html Yamanashi Prefecture Archaeological Museum] (Exhibit on keyhole tombs from their emergence to disappearance) {{Japanese-history-stub}} * [[Isezuka Kofun (Chūō)]] ...Kofun]] ([[kofun]]) located in Takabe, {{ill|Chūō, Yamanashi|ja|中央市|12=draft}}, Yamanashi Prefecture. The tomb is classified as a [[circular burial moun ...gside nearby Kofuns like the {{ill|Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)|ja|王塚古墳 (中央市)|12=draft}}, it constitutes part of the Uyamadaira Kofun Group. The name "Isetsuka" ... 5 KB (599 words) - 22:23, 14 January [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:History of Chūō City]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:City-Historic Site1 historical sites in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:王塚古墳 (中央市)]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)]] [[ja:王塚古墳 (中央市)]] {{citation needed|Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)}} {{Kofun navbar}}

Iwashita, originally from Toyotomi Village, recounted encountering the helmet in 1932 and facilitating its transfer to the Imperial Household Museum (now the Tokyo National Museum). [[File:Western areas Kofu Basin,from the Ouzuka-Kofun.jpg|thumb|left|500px|View of the northwestern Kōfu Basin from Ōzuka Kofun (Photographed in August 2013).]] {{-}} == References == * Hidenori Okano, "Ōzuka Kofun," ''Yamanashi Prefectural History: Archaeology Volume 1'' * Sadawo Iwashita, ''Now I Speak Out: The Horned Helmet Excavated from Ōzuka'', privately published, 2005. * Sukenobu Sueki, "On the Horned Helmet Excavated from Ōzuka Kofun," ''Journal of Yamanashi Archaeological Association, Issue 18,'' 2008. * ''Kai's Black Horse - Horses that shaped History,'' Yamanashi Prefectural Museum, 2014. ** Akihiro Morihara, "Did the Master of Kai-Chōshizuka See Horses?" == notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.chuo.yamanashi.jp/sougou/bunkazai.php?id=36 Yamanashi Prefecture Chūō City Comprehensive Guide: Ōzuka Kofun] * [http://www.manabi.pref.yamanashi.jp/db/servlet/dbview?id=A810000413 Yamanashi Navi Net designation1 Intypeation: Ōzuka Kofun] * [http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/kouko-hak/special/special-08haru.html Yamanashi Prefecture Archaeological Museum] (Exhibit on keyhole tombs from their emergence to disappearance) {{Japanese-history-stub}} * [[Isezuka Kofun (Chūō)]] ...Kofun]] ([[kofun]]) located in Takabe, {{ill|Chūō, Yamanashi|ja|中央市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Yamanashi Prefecture. The tomb is classified as a [[circular burial moun ...gside nearby Kofuns like the {{ill|Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)|ja|王塚古墳 (中央市)|12=draft|12=draft}}, it constitutes part of the Uyamadaira Kofun Group. The name "Isetsuka" ... 5 KB (599 words) - 22:23, 14 January {{draft categories| {{citation needed|Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:History of Chūō City]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:City-Historic Site1 historical sites in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:王塚古墳 (中央市)]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)]] [[ja:王塚古墳 (中央市)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)]][[Category:qq]] }}

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Ōzuka Kofun | location = Ōtorii 4256, Chūō City, Yamanashi Prefecture[http://www.city.chuo.yamanashi.jp/sougou/gappei/data/meeting2_21.pdf Regarding the Handling of Social Education] - Tamaho Town, Tatomi Town, Toyotomi Village Merger Council | coordinates = {{Coord|35|34|46.6|N|138|33|15.1|E|map_size:20000_region:JP-19_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = Keyhole-shaped kofun | built = Early 5th century | area = Diameter: 40 m, Height: 7 m | burial = | burial goods = Iron axe | designation1 = City-Historic Site1 historical site | image = [[File:Ouzuka-Kofun Old tomb Chuo-City from southeastern side.JPG|280px]]
Ōzuka Kofun (Photographed in August 2013) }} [[File:Ouzuka-Kofun Old tomb Chuo-City from west side.JPG|thumb|280px|Ōzuka Kofun, view from the west (Photographed in August 2013)]] '''Ōzuka Kofun''' (王塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun ({{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}) located in {{ill|Chūō City|ja|中央市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Yamanashi Prefecture. Its type is debated as either a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} or a scallop-shaped kofun. == location and Historical Context == Ōtorii in Chūō City lies on the southern edge of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|12=draft|12=draft}}, at the western end of the {{ill|Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵|12=draft|12=draft}}, on a plateau called Uyamahira bordered by the Shichisato and Asari rivers. The site is 342 meters above sea level. This area hosts numerous archaeological sites from the {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代|12=draft|12=draft}} through the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, typeing the Uyamahira Kofun Cluster with other mounds such as {{ill|Futagozuka Kofun|ja|二子塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Isezuka Kofun (Chūō City)|ja|伊勢塚古墳 (中央市)|12=draft|12=draft}}. The mound is scallop-shaped but has also been evaluated as a keyhole-shaped kofun. It measures approximately 64 meters in length and 7 meters in height, and it is estimated to have been constructed in the late 5th century. == Excavations and burial goods == The posterior mound has been subject to looting, and later investigations confirmed a vertical pit-style stone chamber. The tomb was discovered in 1930 (Shōwa 5) and excavated by Yoshio Nishina. It was Historic Site1 a cultural property of {{ill|Toyotomi, Yamanashi|ja|豊富村 (山梨県)|12=draft|12=draft}} in 1970 (Shōwa 45) and is now a city-Historic Site1 cultural property of Chūō City. Excavated burial goods include iron weapons such as swords, knives, and arrowheads, as well as armor, horse fittings, and cylindrical haniwa. The {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft|12=draft}} houses a “{{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|12=draft|12=draft}}” said to have been unearthed in Toyotomi Village. burial goods from the tomb include F-shaped bridles, with other horse fittings from the same era found at the {{ill|Kankan-zuka Kofun|ja|かんかん塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} in the Nakatō Kofun Cluster, part of the Sone Hills, which are considered the oldest horse fittings in the Tokai region from the late 5th centuryMorihara (2014), p.27. Additional burial goods include elliptical bridle pieces from {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster (Ichikawa Misato Town)|ja|大塚古墳群 (市川三郷町)|12=draft|12=draft}}. In Yamanashi Prefecture, horse teeth excavated at the {{ill|Shiobe Ruins|ja|塩部遺跡|12=draft|12=draft}} in Kōfu City and at the {{ill|Higashiyama-kita Ruins|ja|東山北遺跡|12=draft|12=draft}} suggest the arrival of horses in the late 4th century. The construction of small to mid-sized mounds in areas outside the Sone Hills from the late 4th century to the emergence of horse fittings in the 5th century reflects potential social transtypeationsMorihara (2014), p.28. == Significance of Ōzuka Kofun == Ōzuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 5th century based on its burial burial goods. Though not thoroughly investigated, its vertical pit-style stone chamber and scallop-shaped or keyhole-shaped type make it a notable example of ancient tombs in the region. Kofun construction in Yamanashi Prefecture began in the mid-4th century with large tombs like the {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} in the Sone Hills and shifted to smaller-map_size tombs throughout the Kōfu Basin by the mid-to-late 5th century. Ōzuka Kofun is part of this transitional phase, contemporaneous with other mounds such as {{ill|Kitsunezuka Kofun|ja|狐塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} in Fuefuki City and {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun (disambiguation)|ja|大塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} in Ichikawa Misato Town. This period was marked by increased militarization across Japan, driven by geopolitical tensions on the Korean Peninsula and the eastward expansion of the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト王権|12=draft|12=draft}}. Tombs in Yamanashi during this era began to feature military-related burial goods such as armor. == Legacy of the “Horned Helmet” Attributed to Ōzuka Kofun == In 2005 (Heisei 17), a privately published booklet titled ''Now I Speak Out: The Horned Helmet Excavated from Ōzuka'' by Sadawo Iwashita was released. Iwashita, originally from Toyotomi Village, recounted encountering the helmet in 1932 and facilitating its transfer to the Imperial Household Museum (now the Tokyo National Museum). [[File:Western areas Kofu Basin,from the Ouzuka-Kofun.jpg|thumb|left|500px|View of the northwestern Kōfu Basin from Ōzuka Kofun (Photographed in August 2013).]] {{-}} == References == * Hidenori Okano, "Ōzuka Kofun," ''Yamanashi Prefectural History: Archaeology Volume 1'' * Sadawo Iwashita, ''Now I Speak Out: The Horned Helmet Excavated from Ōzuka'', privately published, 2005. * Sukenobu Sueki, "On the Horned Helmet Excavated from Ōzuka Kofun," ''Journal of Yamanashi Archaeological Association, Issue 18,'' 2008. * ''Kai's Black Horse - Horses that shaped History,'' Yamanashi Prefectural Museum, 2014. ** Akihiro Morihara, "Did the Master of Kai-Chōshizuka See Horses?" == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.chuo.yamanashi.jp/sougou/bunkazai.php?id=36 Yamanashi Prefecture Chūō City Comprehensive Guide: Ōzuka Kofun] * [http://www.manabi.pref.yamanashi.jp/db/servlet/dbview?id=A810000413 Yamanashi Navi Net designation1 Intypeation: Ōzuka Kofun] * [http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/kouko-hak/special/special-08haru.html Yamanashi Prefecture Archaeological Museum] (Exhibit on keyhole tombs from their emergence to disappearance) {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:History of Chūō City]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:City-Historic Site1 historical sites in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:王塚古墳 (中央市)]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)]] [[ja:王塚古墳 (中央市)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)]][[Category:qq]] }}

This area hosts numerous archaeological sites from the {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代|12=draft|12=draft}} through the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, typeing the Uyamahira Kofun Cluster with other mounds such as {{ill|Futagozuka Kofun|ja|二子塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Isezuka Kofun (Chūō City)|ja|伊勢塚古墳 (中央市)|12=draft|12=draft}}. The mound is scallop-shaped but has also been evaluated as a keyhole-shaped kofun. It measures approximately 64 meters in length and 7 meters in height, and it is estimated to have been constructed in the late 5th century. == Excavations and burial goods == The posterior mound has been subject to looting, and later investigations confirmed a vertical pit-style stone chamber. The tomb was discovered in 1930 (Shōwa 5) and excavated by Yoshio Nishina. It was Historic Site1 a cultural property of {{ill|Toyotomi, Yamanashi|ja|豊富村 (山梨県)|12=draft|12=draft}} in 1970 (Shōwa 45) and is now a city-Historic Site1 cultural property of Chūō City. Excavated burial goods include iron weapons such as swords, knives, and arrowheads, as well as armor, horse fittings, and cylindrical haniwa. The {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft|12=draft}} houses a “{{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|12=draft|12=draft}}” said to have been unearthed in Toyotomi Village. burial goods from the tomb include F-shaped bridles, with other horse fittings from the same era found at the {{ill|Kankan-zuka Kofun|ja|かんかん塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} in the Nakatō Kofun Cluster, part of the Sone Hills, which are considered the oldest horse fittings in the Tokai region from the late 5th centuryMorihara (2014), p.27. Additional burial goods include elliptical bridle pieces from {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster (Ichikawa Misato Town)|ja|大塚古墳群 (市川三郷町)|12=draft|12=draft}}. In Yamanashi Prefecture, horse teeth excavated at the {{ill|Shiobe Ruins|ja|塩部遺跡|12=draft|12=draft}} in Kōfu City and at the {{ill|Higashiyama-kita Ruins|ja|東山北遺跡|12=draft|12=draft}} suggest the arrival of horses in the late 4th century. The construction of small to mid-sized mounds in areas outside the Sone Hills from the late 4th century to the emergence of horse fittings in the 5th century reflects potential social transtypeationsMorihara (2014), p.28. == Significance of Ōzuka Kofun == Ōzuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 5th century based on its burial burial goods. Though not thoroughly investigated, its vertical pit-style stone chamber and scallop-shaped or keyhole-shaped type make it a notable example of ancient tombs in the region. Kofun construction in Yamanashi Prefecture began in the mid-4th century with large tombs like the {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} in the Sone Hills and shifted to smaller-map_size tombs throughout the Kōfu Basin by the mid-to-late 5th century. Ōzuka Kofun is part of this transitional phase, contemporaneous with other mounds such as {{ill|Kitsunezuka Kofun|ja|狐塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} in Fuefuki City and {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun (disambiguation)|ja|大塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} in Ichikawa Misato Town. This period was marked by increased militarization across Japan, driven by geopolitical tensions on the Korean Peninsula and the eastward expansion of the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト王権|12=draft|12=draft}}. Tombs in Yamanashi during this era began to feature military-related burial goods such as armor. == Legacy of the “Horned Helmet” Attributed to Ōzuka Kofun == In 2005 (Heisei 17), a privately published booklet titled ''Now I Speak Out: The Horned Helmet Excavated from Ōzuka'' by Sadawo Iwashita was released. Iwashita, originally from Toyotomi Village, recounted encountering the helmet in 1932 and facilitating its transfer to the Imperial Household Museum (now the Tokyo National Museum). [[File:Western areas Kofu Basin,from the Ouzuka-Kofun.jpg|thumb|left|500px|View of the northwestern Kōfu Basin from Ōzuka Kofun (Photographed in August 2013).]] {{-}} == References == * Hidenori Okano, "Ōzuka Kofun," ''Yamanashi Prefectural History: Archaeology Volume 1'' * Sadawo Iwashita, ''Now I Speak Out: The Horned Helmet Excavated from Ōzuka'', privately published, 2005. * Sukenobu Sueki, "On the Horned Helmet Excavated from Ōzuka Kofun," ''Journal of Yamanashi Archaeological Association, Issue 18,'' 2008. * ''Kai's Black Horse - Horses that shaped History,'' Yamanashi Prefectural Museum, 2014. ** Akihiro Morihara, "Did the Master of Kai-Chōshizuka See Horses?" == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.chuo.yamanashi.jp/sougou/bunkazai.php?id=36 Yamanashi Prefecture Chūō City Comprehensive Guide: Ōzuka Kofun] * [http://www.manabi.pref.yamanashi.jp/db/servlet/dbview?id=A810000413 Yamanashi Navi Net designation1 Intypeation: Ōzuka Kofun] * [http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/kouko-hak/special/special-08haru.html Yamanashi Prefecture Archaeological Museum] (Exhibit on keyhole tombs from their emergence to disappearance) {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:History of Chūō City]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:City-Historic Site1 historical sites in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:王塚古墳 (中央市)]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)]] [[ja:王塚古墳 (中央市)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)]][[Category:qq]] }}

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Ōzuka Kofun | location = Ōtorii 4256, Chūō City, Yamanashi Prefecture[http://www.city.chuo.yamanashi.jp/sougou/gappei/data/meeting2_21.pdf Regarding the Handling of Social Education] - Tamaho Town, Tatomi Town, Toyotomi Village Merger Council | coordinates = {{Coord|35|34|46.6|N|138|33|15.1|E|map_size:20000_region:JP-19_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = Keyhole-shaped kofun | built = Early 5th century | area = Diameter: 40 m, Height: 7 m | burial = | burial goods = Iron axe | designation1 = City-Historic Site1 historical site | image = [[File:Ouzuka-Kofun Old tomb Chuo-City from southeastern side.JPG|280px]]
Ōzuka Kofun (Photographed in August 2013) }} [[File:Ouzuka-Kofun Old tomb Chuo-City from west side.JPG|thumb|280px|Ōzuka Kofun, view from the west (Photographed in August 2013)]] '''Ōzuka Kofun''' (王塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun ({{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}) located in {{ill|Chūō City|ja|中央市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Yamanashi Prefecture. revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称=王塚古墳 |所在地=山梨県中央市大鳥居4256[http://www.city.chuo.yamanashi.jp/sougou/gappei/data/meeting2_21.pdf 審議第4号 資料 社会教育の取扱いに関すること] - 玉穂町・田富町・豊富村合併協議会 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|34|46.6|N|138|33|15.1|E|scale:20000_region:JP-19_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |形状=前方後円墳 |築造年代=5世紀前半 |規模=直径40m、高さ7m |被葬者= |出土品=鉄斧 |史跡指定=市史跡 |画像=[[File:Ouzuka-Kofun Old tomb Chuo-City from southeastern side.JPG|280px]]
王塚古墳（2013年8月撮影） }} [[File:Ouzuka-Kofun Old tomb Chuo-City from west side.JPG|thumb|280px|王塚古墳。西側から撮影。（2013年8月撮影）]] '''王塚古墳'''（おうづかこふん）は、[[山梨県]][[中央市]]大鳥居に所在する[[古墳]]。形式は[[前方後円墳]]もしくは帆立貝式古墳。 == 立地と地理的・歴史的景観 == 所在する中央市大鳥居は[[甲府盆地]]南縁に位置し、盆地南端に広がる[[曽根丘陵]]の西端にあたり、宇山平と呼ばれる七覚川・浅利川に画された台地西側に立地する。標高は342メートル。一帯は[[縄文時代]]から[[奈良時代|奈良]]・[[平安時代]]にかけての宇山平遺跡が分布するほか、[[二子塚古墳]]や[[伊勢塚古墳 (中央市)|伊勢塚古墳]]等とともに宇山平古墳群を形成している。 [[帆立貝形古墳|帆立貝形]]を呈するが、前方後円墳と評価する説もある。墳丘長は約64メートルで高さは7メートルの規模を有し、[[5世紀]]後半に築造されたと推定される。 == 発掘調査と出土遺物 == 後円部は[[盗掘]]を受けており、のちの調査で竪穴式合掌形石室であることが判明した。[[1930年]]（[[昭和]]5年）に発見され、[[仁科義男]]により[[発掘調査]]が行われる。本古墳は、[[1970年]]（[[昭和]]45年）に[[豊富村 (山梨県)|豊富村]]の文化財に指定され、現在は中央市の市指定文化財となっている。 [[副葬品]]として[[剣]]・[[刀]]・[[鉄鏃]]などの鉄製武具、[[短甲]]（胸背部）・[[馬具]]・[[円筒埴輪]]を出土している。なお、[[東京国立博物館]]には、豊富村出土と伝えられる「[[衝角付冑]]」が収蔵されている。 王塚古墳からはf字形鏡板板付轡が出土している『甲斐の黒駒-歴史を動かした馬たち-』、p.40。山梨県内では[[曽根丘陵]]の中道古墳群に属する[[かんかん塚古墳]]において[[5世紀]]後半の馬具が出土しており、これが最古の馬具とされる森原（2014）、p.27。王塚古墳からはこれ次ぐ5世紀後半代の金銅製馬具が出土している。5世紀代の馬具として[[大塚古墳群 (市川三郷町)|大塚古墳]]（[[市川三郷町]]）から出土した鉄製楕円形鏡板付[[轡]]・鉄製剣菱形葉がある。 山梨県では[[甲府市]]塩部の[[塩部遺跡]]や甲府市下向山の[[東山北遺跡]]から出土した[[馬歯]]の存在から、[[4世紀]]後半から4世紀末にかけて[[馬]]が渡来したと考えられている『甲斐の黒駒-歴史を動かした馬たち-』、p.22。古墳時代の山梨県では、[[甲斐銚子塚古墳]]が築造された曽根丘陵において5世紀初頭の[[丸山塚古墳 (甲府市)|丸山塚古墳]]を最後に大型古墳の築造が停止し、曽根丘陵以外の地域で中小規模の古墳の築造が開始されていることから、ウマが導入された4世紀後半・4世紀末から、馬具が出現する5世紀後半までの時代にかけて、社会的変動が生じたとも考えられている森原（2014）、p.28。 == 王塚古墳の位置づけ == 王塚古墳は出土遺物から5世紀後半の築造と推定され、十分な学術的調査を経ていないが合掌式石室を持つ前方後円墳であると見られる点が特徴とされる。 山梨県における古墳の築造は[[甲斐銚子塚古墳]]に代表される4世紀中盤代に曽根丘陵から開始され、5世紀前半には[[市川三郷町]]大塚の[[鳥居原狐塚古墳]]の築造を経て、5世紀中盤・後半には古墳の築造が甲府盆地各地へ拡散し、王塚古墳の築造はこの時期に位置づけられる。山梨県内における同時期の古墳には笛吹市八代町の狐塚古墳や市川三郷町の大塚古墳、甲府市白井の表門神社古墳が挙げられる。 この時期は列島規模で軍事的色彩が強まった時期で、[[朝鮮半島]]を巡る軍事的緊張が高まり、[[ヤマト王権]]の東国進出も加速した時期とされる。東国である山梨県においてもこうした動向の影響を受け、甲冑など軍事に関わる副葬品を伴う古墳が出現したと考えられている。 == 伝王塚古墳出土「衝角付冑」 == [[2005年]]（[[平成]]17年）には岩下貞男による私家版の小冊子『''今だからもの申そう　王塚出土の衝角付兜等について''』が刊行される。岩下は旧豊富村の出身で、昭和6年に[[宮内省]][[皇宮警察 (宮内省)|皇宮警察]]部に入所した人物で、後に警察長を務めた。岩下は入所した翌昭和7年に帰郷した際に、郷里の先輩から私蔵していた伝王塚古墳出土とされる「衝角付冑」を見せられ、帝室博物館（現在の[[東京国立博物館]]）への収蔵を仲介したという。 [[File:Western areas Kofu Basin,from the Ouzuka-Kofun.jpg|thumb|left|500px|王塚古墳から[[甲府盆地]]北西部方面の眺め。（2013年8月撮影。）]] {{-}} == 参考文献 == * 岡野秀典「王塚古墳」『山梨県史』資料編1原始・古代1考古（遺跡） * 岩下貞男『今だからもの申そう　王塚出土の衝角付兜等について』私家版、2005年 * 末木健「王塚古墳出土衝角付兜について-岩下貞男氏による発見場所の確定-」『山梨県考古学協会誌 第18号』山梨県考古学協会、2008年 * 『甲斐の黒駒-歴史を動かした馬たち-』山梨県立博物館、2014年 **　森原明廣「甲斐銚子塚の主は馬を見たのか」 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} == 外部リンク == * [http://www.city.chuo.yamanashi.jp/sougou/bunkazai.php?id=36 山梨県中央市総合案内「王塚古墳」] * [http://www.manabi.pref.yamanashi.jp/db/servlet/dbview?id=A810000413 やまなしナビネット文化財情報「王塚古墳」] * [http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/kouko-hak/special/special-08haru.html 山梨県／考古博物館]（「平成20年度春季企画展『甲斐の前方後円墳』〜その出現から消滅まで〜」について） {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おうつかこふん}} [[Category:中央市の歴史]] [[Category:山梨県の古墳]] [[Category:山梨県内の市町村指定史跡]] [[Category:前方後円墳]]

王塚古墳（2013年8月撮影） }} [[File:Ouzuka-Kofun Old tomb Chuo-City from west side.JPG|thumb|280px|王塚古墳。西側から撮影。（2013年8月撮影）]] '''王塚古墳'''（おうづかこふん）は、[[山梨県]][[中央市]]大鳥居に所在する[[古墳]]。形式は[[前方後円墳]]もしくは帆立貝式古墳。 == (市川三郷町)|大塚古墳]]（[[市川三郷町]]）から出土した鉄製楕円形鏡板付[[轡]]・鉄製剣菱形葉がある。 (甲府市)|丸山塚古墳]]を最後に大型古墳の築造が停止し、曽根丘陵以外の地域で中小規模の古墳の築造が開始されていることから、ウマが導入された4世紀後半・4世紀末から、馬具が出現する5世紀後半までの時代にかけて、社会的変動が生じたとも考えられている森原（2014）、p.28。 == 王塚古墳の位置づけ == (宮内省)|皇宮警察]]部に入所した人物で、後に警察長を務めた。岩下は入所した翌昭和7年に帰郷した際に、郷里の先輩から私蔵していた伝王塚古墳出土とされる「衝角付冑」を見せられ、帝室博物館（現在の[[東京国立博物館]]）への収蔵を仲介したという。 [[File:Western areas Kofu Basin,from the Ouzuka-Kofun.jpg|thumb|left|500px|王塚古墳から[[甲府盆地]]北西部方面の眺め。（2013年8月撮影。）]] {{-}} == 参考文献 == * 岡野秀典「王塚古墳」『山梨県史』資料編1原始・古代1考古（遺跡） * 岩下貞男『今だからもの申そう　王塚出土の衝角付兜等について』私家版、2005年 * {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Ōzuka Kofun | location = Ōtorii 4256, Chūō City, Yamanashi Prefecture[http://www.city.chuo.yamanashi.jp/sougou/gappei/data/meeting2_21.pdf Regarding the Handling of Social Education] - Tamaho Town, Tatomi Town, Toyotomi Village Merger Council | coordinates = {{Coord|35|34|46.6|N|138|33|15.1|E|map_size:20000_region:JP-19_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = Keyhole-shaped kofun | built = Early 5th century | area = Diameter: 40 m, Height: 7 m | burial = | burial goods = Iron axe | designation1 = City-Historic Site1 historical site | image = [[File:Ouzuka-Kofun Old tomb Chuo-City from southeastern side.JPG|280px]]
Ōzuka Kofun (Photographed in August 2013) }} [[File:Ouzuka-Kofun Old tomb Chuo-City from west side.JPG|thumb|280px|Ōzuka Kofun, view from the west (Photographed in August 2013)]] '''Ōzuka Kofun''' (王塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun ({{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) located in {{ill|Chūō City|ja|中央市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Yamanashi Prefecture. Its type is debated as either a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or a scallop-shaped kofun. == location and Historical Context == Ōtorii in Chūō City lies on the southern edge of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, at the western end of the {{ill|Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, on a plateau called Uyamahira bordered by the Shichisato and Asari rivers. The site is 342 meters above sea level. This area hosts numerous archaeological sites from the {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} through the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, typeing the Uyamahira Kofun Cluster with other mounds such as {{ill|Futagozuka Kofun|ja|二子塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Isezuka Kofun (Chūō City)|ja|伊勢塚古墳 (中央市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The mound is scallop-shaped but has also been evaluated as a keyhole-shaped kofun. It measures approximately 64 meters in length and 7 meters in height, and it is estimated to have been constructed in the late 5th century. == Excavations and burial goods == The posterior mound has been subject to looting, and later investigations confirmed a vertical pit-style stone chamber. The tomb was discovered in 1930 (Shōwa 5) and excavated by Yoshio Nishina. It was Historic Site1 a cultural property of {{ill|Toyotomi, Yamanashi|ja|豊富村 (山梨県)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in 1970 (Shōwa 45) and is now a city-Historic Site1 cultural property of Chūō City. Excavated burial goods include iron weapons such as swords, knives, and arrowheads, as well as armor, horse fittings, and cylindrical haniwa. The {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} houses a “{{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|qq=draft}}” said to have been unearthed in Toyotomi Village. burial goods from the tomb include F-shaped bridles, with other horse fittings from the same era found at the {{ill|Kankan-zuka Kofun|ja|かんかん塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the Nakatō Kofun Cluster, part of the Sone Hills, which are considered the oldest horse fittings in the Tokai region from the late 5th centuryMorihara (2014), p.27. Additional burial goods include elliptical bridle pieces from {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster (Ichikawa Misato Town)|ja|大塚古墳群 (市川三郷町)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In Yamanashi Prefecture, horse teeth excavated at the {{ill|Shiobe Ruins|ja|塩部遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Kōfu City and at the {{ill|Higashiyama-kita Ruins|ja|東山北遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} suggest the arrival of horses in the late 4th century. The construction of small to mid-sized mounds in areas outside the Sone Hills from the late 4th century to the emergence of horse fittings in the 5th century reflects potential social transtypeationsMorihara (2014), p.28. == Significance of Ōzuka Kofun == Ōzuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 5th century based on its burial burial goods. Though not thoroughly investigated, its vertical pit-style stone chamber and scallop-shaped or keyhole-shaped type make it a notable example of ancient tombs in the region. Kofun construction in Yamanashi Prefecture began in the mid-4th century with large tombs like the {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the Sone Hills and shifted to smaller-map_size tombs throughout the Kōfu Basin by the mid-to-late 5th century. Ōzuka Kofun is part of this transitional phase, contemporaneous with other mounds such as {{ill|Kitsunezuka Kofun|ja|狐塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Fuefuki City and {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun (disambiguation)|ja|大塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Ichikawa Misato Town. This period was marked by increased militarization across Japan, driven by geopolitical tensions on the Korean Peninsula and the eastward expansion of the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト王権|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Tombs in Yamanashi during this era began to feature military-related burial goods such as armor. == Legacy of the “Horned Helmet” Attributed to Ōzuka Kofun == In 2005 (Heisei 17), a privately published booklet titled ''Now I Speak Out: The Horned Helmet Excavated from Ōzuka'' by Sadawo Iwashita was released. Iwashita, originally from Toyotomi Village, recounted encountering the helmet in 1932 and facilitating its transfer to the Imperial Household Museum (now the Tokyo National Museum). [[File:Western areas Kofu Basin,from the Ouzuka-Kofun.jpg|thumb|left|500px|View of the northwestern Kōfu Basin from Ōzuka Kofun (Photographed in August 2013).]] {{-}} == References == * Hidenori Okano, "Ōzuka Kofun," ''Yamanashi Prefectural History: Archaeology Volume 1'' * Sadawo Iwashita, ''Now I Speak Out: The Horned Helmet Excavated from Ōzuka'', privately published, 2005. * Sukenobu Sueki, "On the Horned Helmet Excavated from Ōzuka Kofun," ''Journal of Yamanashi Archaeological Association, Issue 18,'' 2008. * ''Kai's Black Horse - Horses that shaped History,'' Yamanashi Prefectural Museum, 2014. ** Akihiro Morihara, "Did the Master of Kai-Chōshizuka See Horses?" == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.chuo.yamanashi.jp/sougou/bunkazai.php?id=36 Yamanashi Prefecture Chūō City Comprehensive Guide: Ōzuka Kofun] * [http://www.manabi.pref.yamanashi.jp/db/servlet/dbview?id=A810000413 Yamanashi Navi Net designation1 Intypeation: Ōzuka Kofun] * [http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/kouko-hak/special/special-08haru.html Yamanashi Prefecture Archaeological Museum] (Exhibit on keyhole tombs from their emergence to disappearance) {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:History of Chūō City]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:City-Historic Site1 historical sites in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:王塚古墳 (中央市)]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)]] [[ja:王塚古墳 (中央市)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)]][[Category:qq]] }} {{translated page|ja|王塚古墳 (中央市)|version=100271305|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Ōzuka Kofun (Photographed in August 2013) }} [[File:Ouzuka-Kofun Old tomb Chuo-City from west side.JPG|thumb|280px|Ōzuka Kofun, view from the west (Photographed in August 2013)]] '''Ōzuka Kofun''' (王塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun ({{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) located in {{ill|Chūō City|ja|中央市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Yamanashi Prefecture. Its type is debated as either a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or a scallop-shaped kofun. == location and Historical Context == Ōtorii in Chūō City lies on the southern edge of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, at the western end of the {{ill|Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, on a plateau called Uyamahira bordered by the Shichisato and Asari rivers. The site is 342 meters above sea level. This area hosts numerous archaeological sites from the {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} through the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, typeing the Uyamahira Kofun Cluster with other mounds such as {{ill|Futagozuka Kofun|ja|二子塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Isezuka Kofun (Chūō City)|ja|伊勢塚古墳 (中央市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The mound is scallop-shaped but has also been evaluated as a keyhole-shaped kofun. It measures approximately 64 meters in length and 7 meters in height, and it is estimated to have been constructed in the late 5th century. == Excavations and burial goods == The posterior mound has been subject to looting, and later investigations confirmed a vertical pit-style stone chamber. The tomb was discovered in 1930 (Shōwa 5) and excavated by Yoshio Nishina. It was Historic Site1 a cultural property of {{ill|Toyotomi, Yamanashi|ja|豊富村 (山梨県)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in 1970 (Shōwa 45) and is now a city-Historic Site1 cultural property of Chūō City. The {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} houses a “{{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|qq=draft}}” said to have been unearthed in Toyotomi Village. burial goods from the tomb include F-shaped bridles, with other horse fittings from the same era found at the {{ill|Kankan-zuka Kofun|ja|かんかん塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the Nakatō Kofun Cluster, part of the Sone Hills, which are considered the oldest horse fittings in the Tokai region from the late 5th centuryMorihara (2014), p.27. Additional burial goods include elliptical bridle pieces from {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster (Ichikawa Misato Town)|ja|大塚古墳群 (市川三郷町)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In Yamanashi Prefecture, horse teeth excavated at the {{ill|Shiobe Ruins|ja|塩部遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Kōfu City and at the {{ill|Higashiyama-kita Ruins|ja|東山北遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} suggest the arrival of horses in the late 4th century. The construction of small to mid-sized mounds in areas outside the Sone Hills from the late 4th century to the emergence of horse fittings in the 5th century reflects potential social transtypeationsMorihara (2014), p.28. == Significance of Ōzuka Kofun == Ōzuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 5th century based on its burial burial goods. Though not thoroughly investigated, its vertical pit-style stone chamber and scallop-shaped or keyhole-shaped type make it a notable example of ancient tombs in the region. Kofun construction in Yamanashi Prefecture began in the mid-4th century with large tombs like the {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the Sone Hills and shifted to smaller-map_size tombs throughout the Kōfu Basin by the mid-to-late 5th century. Ōzuka Kofun is part of this transitional phase, contemporaneous with other mounds such as {{ill|Kitsunezuka Kofun|ja|狐塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Fuefuki City and {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun (disambiguation)|ja|大塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Ichikawa Misato Town. This period was marked by increased militarization across Japan, driven by geopolitical tensions on the Korean Peninsula and the eastward expansion of the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト王権|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Tombs in Yamanashi during this era began to feature military-related burial goods such as armor. == Legacy of the “Horned Helmet” Attributed to Ōzuka Kofun == In 2005 (Heisei 17), a privately published booklet titled ''Now I Speak Out: The Horned Helmet Excavated from Ōzuka'' by Sadawo Iwashita was released. Iwashita, originally from Toyotomi Village, recounted encountering the helmet in 1932 and facilitating its transfer to the Imperial Household Museum (now the Tokyo National Museum). [[File:Western areas Kofu Basin,from the Ouzuka-Kofun.jpg|thumb|left|500px|View of the northwestern Kōfu Basin from Ōzuka Kofun (Photographed in August 2013).]] {{-}} == References == * Hidenori Okano, "Ōzuka Kofun," ''Yamanashi Prefectural History: Archaeology Volume 1'' * Sadawo Iwashita, ''Now I Speak Out: The Horned Helmet Excavated from Ōzuka'', privately published, 2005. * Sukenobu Sueki, "On the Horned Helmet Excavated from Ōzuka Kofun," ''Journal of Yamanashi Archaeological Association, Issue 18,'' 2008. * ''Kai's Black Horse - Horses that shaped History,'' Yamanashi Prefectural Museum, 2014. ** Akihiro Morihara, "Did the Master of Kai-Chōshizuka See Horses?" == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.chuo.yamanashi.jp/sougou/bunkazai.php?id=36 Yamanashi Prefecture Chūō City Comprehensive Guide: Ōzuka Kofun] * [http://www.manabi.pref.yamanashi.jp/db/servlet/dbview?id=A810000413 Yamanashi Navi Net designation1 Intypeation: Ōzuka Kofun] * [http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/kouko-hak/special/special-08haru.html Yamanashi Prefecture Archaeological Museum] (Exhibit on keyhole tombs from their emergence to disappearance) {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City) }} {{translated page|ja|王塚古墳 (中央市)|version=100271305|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:History of Chūō City]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:City-Historic Site1 historical sites in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:王塚古墳 (中央市)]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)]] [[ja:王塚古墳 (中央市)]]

Ōzuka Kofun (Photographed in August 2013) }} [[File:Ouzuka-Kofun Old tomb Chuo-City from west side.JPG|thumb|280px|Ōzuka Kofun, view from the west (Photographed in August 2013)]] '''Ōzuka Kofun''' (王塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun ({{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) located in {{ill|Chūō City|ja|中央市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Yamanashi Prefecture. Its type is debated as either a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or a scallop-shaped kofun. == location and Historical Context == Ōtorii in Chūō City lies on the southern edge of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, at the western end of the {{ill|Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, on a plateau called Uyamahira bordered by the Shichisato and Asari rivers. The site is 342 meters above sea level. This area hosts numerous archaeological sites from the {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} through the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, typeing the Uyamahira Kofun Cluster with other mounds such as {{ill|Futagozuka Kofun|ja|二子塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Isezuka Kofun (Chūō City)|ja|伊勢塚古墳 (中央市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The mound is scallop-shaped but has also been evaluated as a keyhole-shaped kofun. It measures approximately 64 meters in length and 7 meters in height, and it is estimated to have been constructed in the late 5th century. == Excavations and burial goods == The posterior mound has been subject to looting, and later investigations confirmed a vertical pit-style stone chamber. The tomb was discovered in 1930 (Shōwa 5) and excavated by Yoshio Nishina. It was Historic Site1 a cultural property of {{ill|Toyotomi, Yamanashi|ja|豊富村 (山梨県)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in 1970 (Shōwa 45) and is now a city-Historic Site1 cultural property of Chūō City. Excavated burial goods include iron weapons such as swords, knives, and arrowheads, as well as armor, horse fittings, and cylindrical haniwa. The {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} houses a “{{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|qq=draft}}” said to have been unearthed in Toyotomi Village. burial goods from the tomb include F-shaped bridles, with other horse fittings from the same era found at the {{ill|Kankan-zuka Kofun|ja|かんかん塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the Nakatō Kofun Cluster, part of the Sone Hills, which are considered the oldest horse fittings in the Tokai region from the late 5th centuryMorihara (2014), p.27. Additional burial goods include elliptical bridle pieces from {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster (Ichikawa Misato Town)|ja|大塚古墳群 (市川三郷町)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In Yamanashi Prefecture, horse teeth excavated at the {{ill|Shiobe Ruins|ja|塩部遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Kōfu City and at the {{ill|Higashiyama-kita Ruins|ja|東山北遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} suggest the arrival of horses in the late 4th century. The construction of small to mid-sized mounds in areas outside the Sone Hills from the late 4th century to the emergence of horse fittings in the 5th century reflects potential social transtypeationsMorihara (2014), p.28. == Significance of Ōzuka Kofun == Ōzuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 5th century based on its burial burial goods. Though not thoroughly investigated, its vertical pit-style stone chamber and scallop-shaped or keyhole-shaped type make it a notable example of ancient tombs in the region. Kofun construction in Yamanashi Prefecture began in the mid-4th century with large tombs like the {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the Sone Hills and shifted to smaller-map_size tombs throughout the Kōfu Basin by the mid-to-late 5th century. Ōzuka Kofun is part of this transitional phase, contemporaneous with other mounds such as {{ill|Kitsunezuka Kofun|ja|狐塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Fuefuki City and {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun (disambiguation)|ja|大塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Ichikawa Misato Town. This period was marked by increased militarization across Japan, driven by geopolitical tensions on the Korean Peninsula and the eastward expansion of the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト王権|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Tombs in Yamanashi during this era began to feature military-related burial goods such as armor. == Legacy of the “Horned Helmet” Attributed to Ōzuka Kofun == In 2005 (Heisei 17), a privately published booklet titled ''Now I Speak Out: The Horned Helmet Excavated from Ōzuka'' by Sadawo Iwashita was released. Iwashita, originally from Toyotomi Village, recounted encountering the helmet in 1932 and facilitating its transfer to the Imperial Household Museum (now the Tokyo National Museum). [[File:Western areas Kofu Basin,from the Ouzuka-Kofun.jpg|thumb|left|500px|View of the northwestern Kōfu Basin from Ōzuka Kofun (Photographed in August 2013).]] {{-}} == References == * Hidenori Okano, "Ōzuka Kofun," ''Yamanashi Prefectural History: Archaeology Volume 1'' * Sadawo Iwashita, ''Now I Speak Out: The Horned Helmet Excavated from Ōzuka'', privately published, 2005. * Sukenobu Sueki, "On the Horned Helmet Excavated from Ōzuka Kofun," ''Journal of Yamanashi Archaeological Association, Issue 18,'' 2008. * ''Kai's Black Horse - Horses that shaped History,'' Yamanashi Prefectural Museum, 2014. ** Akihiro Morihara, "Did the Master of Kai-Chōshizuka See Horses?" == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.chuo.yamanashi.jp/sougou/bunkazai.php?id=36 Yamanashi Prefecture Chūō City Comprehensive Guide: Ōzuka Kofun] * [http://www.manabi.pref.yamanashi.jp/db/servlet/dbview?id=A810000413 Yamanashi Navi Net designation1 Intypeation: Ōzuka Kofun] * [http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/kouko-hak/special/special-08haru.html Yamanashi Prefecture Archaeological Museum] (Exhibit on keyhole tombs from their emergence to disappearance) {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|王塚古墳 (中央市)|version=100271305|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:History of Chūō City]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:City-Historic Site1 historical sites in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:王塚古墳 (中央市)]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)]] [[ja:王塚古墳 (中央市)]]

[[File:Western areas Kofu Basin,from the Ouzuka-Kofun.jpg|thumb|left|500px|View of the northwestern Kōfu Basin from Ōzuka Kofun (Photographed in August 2013).]] {{-}} == References == * Hidenori Okano, "Ōzuka Kofun," ''Yamanashi Prefectural History: Archaeology Volume 1'' * Sadawo Iwashita, ''Now I Speak Out: The Horned Helmet Excavated from Ōzuka'', privately published, 2005. * Sukenobu Sueki, "On the Horned Helmet Excavated from Ōzuka Kofun," ''Journal of Yamanashi Archaeological Association, Issue 18,'' 2008. * ''Kai's Black Horse - Horses that shaped History,'' Yamanashi Prefectural Museum, 2014. ** Akihiro Morihara, "Did the Master of Kai-Chōshizuka See Horses?" == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.chuo.yamanashi.jp/sougou/bunkazai.php?id=36 Yamanashi Prefecture Chūō City Comprehensive Guide: Ōzuka Kofun] * [http://www.manabi.pref.yamanashi.jp/db/servlet/dbview?id=A810000413 Yamanashi Navi Net designation1 Intypeation: Ōzuka Kofun] * [http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/kouko-hak/special/special-08haru.html Yamanashi Prefecture Archaeological Museum] (Exhibit on keyhole tombs from their emergence to disappearance) {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|王塚古墳 (中央市)|version=100271305|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:History of Chūō City]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:City-Historic Site1 historical sites in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:王塚古墳 (中央市)]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)]] [[ja:王塚古墳 (中央市)]]

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Ōzuka Kofun | location = Ōtorii 4256, Chūō City, Yamanashi Prefecture[http://www.city.chuo.yamanashi.jp/sougou/gappei/data/meeting2_21.pdf Regarding the Handling of Social Education] - Tamaho Town, Tatomi Town, Toyotomi Village Merger Council | coordinates = {{Coord|35|34|46.6|N|138|33|15.1|E|map_size:20000_region:JP-19_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = Keyhole-shaped kofun | built = Early 5th century | area = Diameter: 40 m, Height: 7 m | burial = | burial goods = Iron axe | designation1 = City-Historic Site1 historical site | image = [[File:Ouzuka-Kofun Old tomb Chuo-City from southeastern side.JPG|280px]]
Ōzuka Kofun (Photographed in August 2013) }} [[File:Ouzuka-Kofun Old tomb Chuo-City from west side.JPG|thumb|280px|Ōzuka Kofun, view from the west (Photographed in August 2013)]] '''Ōzuka Kofun''' (王塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun ({{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) located in {{ill|Chūō City|ja|中央市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Yamanashi Prefecture. Its type is debated as either a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or a scallop-shaped kofun. == location and Historical Context == Ōtorii in Chūō City lies on the southern edge of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, at the western end of the {{ill|Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, on a plateau called Uyamahira bordered by the Shichisato and Asari rivers. The site is 342 meters above sea level. This area hosts numerous archaeological sites from the {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} through the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, typeing the Uyamahira Kofun Cluster with other mounds such as {{ill|Futagozuka Kofun|ja|二子塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Isezuka Kofun (Chūō City)|ja|伊勢塚古墳 (中央市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The mound is scallop-shaped but has also been evaluated as a keyhole-shaped kofun. It measures approximately 64 meters in length and 7 meters in height, and it is estimated to have been constructed in the late 5th century. == Excavations and burial goods == The posterior mound has been subject to looting, and later investigations confirmed a vertical pit-style stone chamber. The tomb was discovered in 1930 (Shōwa 5) and excavated by Yoshio Nishina. It was Historic Site1 a cultural property of {{ill|Toyotomi, Yamanashi|ja|豊富村 (山梨県)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in 1970 (Shōwa 45) and is now a city-Historic Site1 cultural property of Chūō City. Excavated burial goods include iron weapons such as swords, knives, and arrowheads, as well as armor, horse fittings, and cylindrical haniwa. The {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} houses a “{{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|qq=draft}}” said to have been unearthed in Toyotomi Village. burial goods from the tomb include F-shaped bridles, with other horse fittings from the same era found at the {{ill|Kankan-zuka Kofun|ja|かんかん塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the Nakatō Kofun Cluster, part of the Sone Hills, which are considered the oldest horse fittings in the Tokai region from the late 5th centuryMorihara (2014), p.27. Additional burial goods include elliptical bridle pieces from {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster (Ichikawa Misato Town)|ja|大塚古墳群 (市川三郷町)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In Yamanashi Prefecture, horse teeth excavated at the {{ill|Shiobe Ruins|ja|塩部遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Kōfu City and at the {{ill|Higashiyama-kita Ruins|ja|東山北遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} suggest the arrival of horses in the late 4th century. The construction of small to mid-sized mounds in areas outside the Sone Hills from the late 4th century to the emergence of horse fittings in the 5th century reflects potential social transtypeationsMorihara (2014), p.28. == Significance of Ōzuka Kofun == Ōzuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 5th century based on its burial burial goods. Though not thoroughly investigated, its vertical pit-style stone chamber and scallop-shaped or keyhole-shaped type make it a notable example of ancient tombs in the region. Kofun construction in Yamanashi Prefecture began in the mid-4th century with large tombs like the {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the Sone Hills and shifted to smaller-map_size tombs throughout the Kōfu Basin by the mid-to-late 5th century. Ōzuka Kofun is part of this transitional phase, contemporaneous with other mounds such as {{ill|Kitsunezuka Kofun|ja|狐塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Fuefuki City and {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun (disambiguation)|ja|大塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Ichikawa Misato Town. This period was marked by increased militarization across Japan, driven by geopolitical tensions on the Korean Peninsula and the eastward expansion of the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト王権|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Tombs in Yamanashi during this era began to feature military-related burial goods such as armor. == Legacy of the “Horned Helmet” Attributed to Ōzuka Kofun == In 2005 (Heisei 17), a privately published booklet titled ''Now I Speak Out: The Horned Helmet Excavated from Ōzuka'' by Sadawo Iwashita was released. Iwashita, originally from Toyotomi Village, recounted encountering the helmet in 1932 and facilitating its transfer to the Imperial Household Museum (now the Tokyo National Museum). [[File:Western areas Kofu Basin,from the Ouzuka-Kofun.jpg|thumb|left|500px|View of the northwestern Kōfu Basin from Ōzuka Kofun (Photographed in August 2013).]] {{-}} == References == * Hidenori Okano, "Ōzuka Kofun," ''Yamanashi Prefectural History: Archaeology Volume 1'' * Sadawo Iwashita, ''Now I Speak Out: The Horned Helmet Excavated from Ōzuka'', privately published, 2005. * Sukenobu Sueki, "On the Horned Helmet Excavated from Ōzuka Kofun," ''Journal of Yamanashi Archaeological Association, Issue 18,'' 2008. * ''Kai's Black Horse - Horses that shaped History,'' Yamanashi Prefectural Museum, 2014. ** Akihiro Morihara, "Did the Master of Kai-Chōshizuka See Horses?" == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.chuo.yamanashi.jp/sougou/bunkazai.php?id=36 Yamanashi Prefecture Chūō City Comprehensive Guide: Ōzuka Kofun] * [http://www.manabi.pref.yamanashi.jp/db/servlet/dbview?id=A810000413 Yamanashi Navi Net designation1 Intypeation: Ōzuka Kofun] * [http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/kouko-hak/special/special-08haru.html Yamanashi Prefecture Archaeological Museum] (Exhibit on keyhole tombs from their emergence to disappearance) {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|王塚古墳 (中央市)|version=100271305|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:History of Chūō City]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:City-Historic Site1 historical sites in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:王塚古墳 (中央市)]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)]] [[ja:王塚古墳 (中央市)]]

Ōzuka Kofun (Photographed in August 2013) }} [[File:Ouzuka-Kofun Old tomb Chuo-City from west side.JPG|thumb|280px|Ōzuka Kofun, view from the west (Photographed in August 2013)]] '''Ōzuka Kofun''' (王塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun ({{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}}) located in {{ill|Chūō City|ja|中央市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūō City|qq=}}, Yamanashi Prefecture. Its type is debated as either a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} or a scallop-shaped kofun. == location and Historical Context == Ōtorii in Chūō City lies on the southern edge of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōfu Basin|qq=}}, at the western end of the {{ill|Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sone Hills|qq=}}, on a plateau called Uyamahira bordered by the Shichisato and Asari rivers. The site is 342 meters above sea level. This area hosts numerous archaeological sites from the {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōmon period|qq=}} through the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara period|qq=}} and {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian period|qq=}}, typeing the Uyamahira Kofun Cluster with other mounds such as {{ill|Futagozuka Kofun|ja|二子塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futagozuka Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Isezuka Kofun (Chūō City)|ja|伊勢塚古墳 (中央市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isezuka Kofun (Chūō City)|qq=}}. The mound is scallop-shaped but has also been evaluated as a keyhole-shaped kofun. It measures approximately 64 meters in length and 7 meters in height, and it is estimated to have been constructed in the late 5th century. == Excavations and burial goods == The posterior mound has been subject to looting, and later investigations confirmed a vertical pit-style stone chamber. The tomb was discovered in 1930 (Shōwa 5) and excavated by Yoshio Nishina. It was Historic Site1 a cultural property of {{ill|Toyotomi, Yamanashi|ja|豊富村 (山梨県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi, Yamanashi|qq=}} in 1970 (Shōwa 45) and is now a city-Historic Site1 cultural property of Chūō City. Excavated burial goods include iron weapons such as swords, knives, and arrowheads, as well as armor, horse fittings, and cylindrical haniwa. The {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo National Museum|qq=}} houses a “{{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horned helmet|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}}” said to have been unearthed in Toyotomi Village. burial goods from the tomb include F-shaped bridles, with other horse fittings from the same era found at the {{ill|Kankan-zuka Kofun|ja|かんかん塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kankan-zuka Kofun|qq=}} in the Nakatō Kofun Cluster, part of the Sone Hills, which are considered the oldest horse fittings in the Tokai region from the late 5th centuryMorihara (2014), p.27. Additional burial goods include elliptical bridle pieces from {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster (Ichikawa Misato Town)|ja|大塚古墳群 (市川三郷町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster (Ichikawa Misato Town)|qq=}}. In Yamanashi Prefecture, horse teeth excavated at the {{ill|Shiobe Ruins|ja|塩部遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobe Ruins|qq=}} in Kōfu City and at the {{ill|Higashiyama-kita Ruins|ja|東山北遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashiyama-kita Ruins|qq=}} suggest the arrival of horses in the late 4th century. The construction of small to mid-sized mounds in areas outside the Sone Hills from the late 4th century to the emergence of horse fittings in the 5th century reflects potential social transtypeationsMorihara (2014), p.28. == Significance of Ōzuka Kofun == Ōzuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 5th century based on its burial burial goods. Though not thoroughly investigated, its vertical pit-style stone chamber and scallop-shaped or keyhole-shaped type make it a notable example of ancient tombs in the region. Kofun construction in Yamanashi Prefecture began in the mid-4th century with large tombs like the {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|qq=}} in the Sone Hills and shifted to smaller-map_size tombs throughout the Kōfu Basin by the mid-to-late 5th century. Ōzuka Kofun is part of this transitional phase, contemporaneous with other mounds such as {{ill|Kitsunezuka Kofun|ja|狐塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsunezuka Kofun|qq=}} in Fuefuki City and {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun (disambiguation)|ja|大塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun (disambiguation)|qq=}} in Ichikawa Misato Town. This period was marked by increased militarization across Japan, driven by geopolitical tensions on the Korean Peninsula and the eastward expansion of the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト王権|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Kingship|qq=}}. Tombs in Yamanashi during this era began to feature military-related burial goods such as armor. == Legacy of the “Horned Helmet” Attributed to Ōzuka Kofun == In 2005 (Heisei 17), a privately published booklet titled ''Now I Speak Out: The Horned Helmet Excavated from Ōzuka'' by Sadawo Iwashita was released. Iwashita, originally from Toyotomi Village, recounted encountering the helmet in 1932 and facilitating its transfer to the Imperial Household Museum (now the Tokyo National Museum). [[File:Western areas Kofu Basin,from the Ouzuka-Kofun.jpg|thumb|left|500px|View of the northwestern Kōfu Basin from Ōzuka Kofun (Photographed in August 2013).]] {{-}} == References == * Hidenori Okano, "Ōzuka Kofun," ''Yamanashi Prefectural History: Archaeology Volume 1'' * Sadawo Iwashita, ''Now I Speak Out: The Horned Helmet Excavated from Ōzuka'', privately published, 2005. * Sukenobu Sueki, "On the Horned Helmet Excavated from Ōzuka Kofun," ''Journal of Yamanashi Archaeological Association, Issue 18,'' 2008. * ''Kai's Black Horse - Horses that shaped History,'' Yamanashi Prefectural Museum, 2014. ** Akihiro Morihara, "Did the Master of Kai-Chōshizuka See Horses?" == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.chuo.yamanashi.jp/sougou/bunkazai.php?id=36 Yamanashi Prefecture Chūō City Comprehensive Guide: Ōzuka Kofun] * [http://www.manabi.pref.yamanashi.jp/db/servlet/dbview?id=A810000413 Yamanashi Navi Net designation1 Intypeation: Ōzuka Kofun] * [http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/kouko-hak/special/special-08haru.html Yamanashi Prefecture Archaeological Museum] (Exhibit on keyhole tombs from their emergence to disappearance) {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|王塚古墳 (中央市)|version=100271305|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:History of Chūō City]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:City-Historic Site1 historical sites in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:王塚古墳 (中央市)]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)]] [[ja:王塚古墳 (中央市)]]

Excavated burial goods include iron weapons such as swords, knives, and arrowheads, as well as armor, horse fittings, and cylindrical haniwa. The {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo National Museum|qq=}} houses a “{{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horned helmet|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}}” said to have been unearthed in Toyotomi Village. burial goods from the tomb include F-shaped bridles, with other horse fittings from the same era found at the {{ill|Kankan-zuka Kofun|ja|かんかん塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kankan-zuka Kofun|qq=}} in the Nakatō Kofun Cluster, part of the Sone Hills, which are considered the oldest horse fittings in the Tokai region from the late 5th centuryMorihara (2014), p.27. Additional burial goods include elliptical bridle pieces from {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster (Ichikawa Misato Town)|ja|大塚古墳群 (市川三郷町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster (Ichikawa Misato Town)|qq=}}. In Yamanashi Prefecture, horse teeth excavated at the {{ill|Shiobe Ruins|ja|塩部遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobe Ruins|qq=}} in Kōfu City and at the {{ill|Higashiyama-kita Ruins|ja|東山北遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashiyama-kita Ruins|qq=}} suggest the arrival of horses in the late 4th century. The construction of small to mid-sized mounds in areas outside the Sone Hills from the late 4th century to the emergence of horse fittings in the 5th century reflects potential social transtypeationsMorihara (2014), p.28. == Significance of Ōzuka Kofun == Ōzuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late 5th century based on its burial burial goods. Though not thoroughly investigated, its vertical pit-style stone chamber and scallop-shaped or keyhole-shaped type make it a notable example of ancient tombs in the region. Kofun construction in Yamanashi Prefecture began in the mid-4th century with large tombs like the {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|qq=}} in the Sone Hills and shifted to smaller-map_size tombs throughout the Kōfu Basin by the mid-to-late 5th century. Ōzuka Kofun is part of this transitional phase, contemporaneous with other mounds such as {{ill|Kitsunezuka Kofun|ja|狐塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsunezuka Kofun|qq=}} in Fuefuki City and {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun (disambiguation)|ja|大塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun (disambiguation)|qq=}} in Ichikawa Misato Town. This period was marked by increased militarization across Japan, driven by geopolitical tensions on the Korean Peninsula and the eastward expansion of the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト王権|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Kingship|qq=}}. Tombs in Yamanashi during this era began to feature military-related burial goods such as armor. == Legacy of the “Horned Helmet” Attributed to Ōzuka Kofun == In 2005 (Heisei 17), a privately published booklet titled ''Now I Speak Out: The Horned Helmet Excavated from Ōzuka'' by Sadawo Iwashita was released. Iwashita, originally from Toyotomi Village, recounted encountering the helmet in 1932 and facilitating its transfer to the Imperial Household Museum (now the Tokyo National Museum). [[File:Western areas Kofu Basin,from the Ouzuka-Kofun.jpg|thumb|left|500px|View of the northwestern Kōfu Basin from Ōzuka Kofun (Photographed in August 2013).]] {{-}} == References == * Hidenori Okano, "Ōzuka Kofun," ''Yamanashi Prefectural History: Archaeology Volume 1'' * Sadawo Iwashita, ''Now I Speak Out: The Horned Helmet Excavated from Ōzuka'', privately published, 2005. * Sukenobu Sueki, "On the Horned Helmet Excavated from Ōzuka Kofun," ''Journal of Yamanashi Archaeological Association, Issue 18,'' 2008. * ''Kai's Black Horse - Horses that shaped History,'' Yamanashi Prefectural Museum, 2014. ** Akihiro Morihara, "Did the Master of Kai-Chōshizuka See Horses?" == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.chuo.yamanashi.jp/sougou/bunkazai.php?id=36 Yamanashi Prefecture Chūō City Comprehensive Guide: Ōzuka Kofun] * [http://www.manabi.pref.yamanashi.jp/db/servlet/dbview?id=A810000413 Yamanashi Navi Net designation1 Intypeation: Ōzuka Kofun] * [http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/kouko-hak/special/special-08haru.html Yamanashi Prefecture Archaeological Museum] (Exhibit on keyhole tombs from their emergence to disappearance) {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|王塚古墳 (中央市)|version=100271305|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:History of Chūō City]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:City-Historic Site1 historical sites in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:王塚古墳 (中央市)]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Ōzuka Kofun (Chūō City)]] [[ja:王塚古墳 (中央市)]]
